
NAME
    Enable-PodeOpenApi
    
SYNOPSIS
    Enables the OpenAPI default route in Pode.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Enable-PodeOpenApi [-Path <String>] [-Title <String>] [-Version <String>] [-Description <String>] 
    [-OpenApiVersion <String>] [-RouteFilter <String>] [-EndpointName <String[]>] [-Middleware <Object[]>] 
    [-Authentication <String>] [-Role <String[]>] [-Group <String[]>] [-Scope <String[]>] [-RestrictRoutes] 
    [-Mode <String>] [-MarkupLanguage <String>] [-EnableSchemaValidation] [-Depth <Int32>] 
    [-DisableMinimalDefinitions] [-DefinitionTag <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Enable-PodeOpenApi [-Path <String>] [-OpenApiVersion <String>] [-RouteFilter <String>] [-EndpointName 
    <String[]>] [-Middleware <Object[]>] [-Authentication <String>] [-Role <String[]>] [-Group <String[]>] 
    [-Scope <String[]>] [-RestrictRoutes] [-Mode <String>] [-MarkupLanguage <String>] 
    [-EnableSchemaValidation] [-Depth <Int32>] [-DisableMinimalDefinitions] -DefaultResponses <Hashtable> 
    [-DefinitionTag <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Enable-PodeOpenApi [-Path <String>] [-OpenApiVersion <String>] [-RouteFilter <String>] [-EndpointName 
    <String[]>] [-Middleware <Object[]>] [-Authentication <String>] [-Role <String[]>] [-Group <String[]>] 
    [-Scope <String[]>] [-RestrictRoutes] [-Mode <String>] [-MarkupLanguage <String>] 
    [-EnableSchemaValidation] [-Depth <Int32>] [-DisableMinimalDefinitions] -NoDefaultResponses 
    [-DefinitionTag <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Enables the OpenAPI default route in Pode, as well as setting up details like Title and API Version.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Path <String>
        An optional custom route path to access the OpenAPI definition. (Default: /openapi)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                /openapi
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Title <String>
        The Title of the API. (Deprecated -  Use Add-PodeOAInfo)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Version <String>
        The Version of the API.   (Deprecated -  Use Add-PodeOAInfo)
        The OpenAPI Specification is versioned using Semantic Versioning 2.0.0 (semver) and follows the 
        semver specification.
        https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Description <String>
        A short description of the API. (Deprecated -  Use Add-PodeOAInfo)
        CommonMark syntax MAY be used for rich text representation.
        https://spec.commonmark.org/
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -OpenApiVersion <String>
        Specify OpenApi Version (default: 3.0.3)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                3.0.3
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -RouteFilter <String>
        An optional route filter for routes that should be included in the definition. (Default: /*)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                /*
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EndpointName <String[]>
        The EndpointName of an Endpoint(s) to bind the static Route against.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Middleware <Object[]>
        Like normal Routes, an array of Middleware that will be applied to the route.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Authentication <String>
        The name of an Authentication method which should be used as middleware on this Route.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Role <String[]>
        One or more optional Roles that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Group <String[]>
        One or more optional Groups that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Scope <String[]>
        One or more optional Scopes that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -RestrictRoutes [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, only routes that are available on the Requests URI will be used to generate the OpenAPI 
        definition.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Mode <String>
        Define the way the OpenAPI definition file is accessed, the value can be View or Download. (Default: 
        View)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                view
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -MarkupLanguage <String>
        Define the default markup language for the OpenApi spec ('Json', 'Json-Compress', 'Yaml')
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                Json
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EnableSchemaValidation [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied enable Test-PodeOAJsonSchemaCompliance  cmdlet that provide support for opeapi parameter 
        schema validation
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Depth <Int32>
        Define the default  depth used by any JSON,YAML OpenAPI conversion (default 20)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                20
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DisableMinimalDefinitions [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied the OpenApi decument will include only the route validated by Set-PodeOARouteInfo. Any 
        other not OpenApi route will be excluded.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DefaultResponses <Hashtable>
        If supplied, it will replace the default OpenAPI response with the new provided.(Default: @{'200' = 
        @{ description = 'OK' };'default' = @{ description = 'Internal server error' }} )
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -NoDefaultResponses [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, it will disable the default OpenAPI response with the new provided.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DefinitionTag <String>
        A string representing the unique tag for the API specification.
        This tag helps distinguish between different versions or types of API specifications within the 
        application.
        You can use this tag to reference the specific API documentation, schema, or version that your 
        function interacts with.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enable-PodeOpenApi -Title 'My API' -Version '1.0.0' -RouteFilter '/api/*'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enable-PodeOpenApi -Title 'My API' -Version '1.0.0' -RouteFilter '/api/*' -RestrictRoutes
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enable-PodeOpenApi -Path '/docs/openapi'   -NoCompress -Mode 'Donwload' -DisableMinimalDefinitions
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Enable-PodeOAViewer
    
SYNOPSIS
    Adds a route that enables a viewer to display OpenAPI docs, such as Swagger, ReDoc, RapiDoc, StopLight, 
    Explorer, RapiPdf or Bookmarks.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Enable-PodeOAViewer -Type <String> [-Path <String>] [-OpenApiUrl <String>] [-Middleware <Object[]>] 
    [-Title <String>] [-DarkMode] [-EndpointName <String[]>] [-Authentication <String>] [-Role <String[]>] 
    [-Group <String[]>] [-Scope <String[]>] [-DefinitionTag <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Enable-PodeOAViewer [-Path <String>] [-OpenApiUrl <String>] [-Middleware <Object[]>] [-Title <String>] 
    [-DarkMode] [-EndpointName <String[]>] [-Authentication <String>] [-Role <String[]>] [-Group <String[]>] 
    [-Scope <String[]>] -Bookmarks [-NoAdvertise] [-DefinitionTag <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Enable-PodeOAViewer [-Path <String>] [-OpenApiUrl <String>] [-Middleware <Object[]>] [-Title <String>] 
    [-DarkMode] [-EndpointName <String[]>] [-Authentication <String>] [-Role <String[]>] [-Group <String[]>] 
    [-Scope <String[]>] -Editor [-DefinitionTag <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Adds a route that enables a viewer to display OpenAPI docs, such as Swagger, ReDoc, RapiDoc, StopLight, 
    Explorer, RapiPdf  or Bookmarks.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Type <String>
        The Type of OpenAPI viewer to use.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Path <String>
        The route Path where the docs can be accessed. (Default: "/$Type")
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -OpenApiUrl <String>
        The URL where the OpenAPI definition can be retrieved. (Default is the OpenAPI path from 
        Enable-PodeOpenApi)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Middleware <Object[]>
        Like normal Routes, an array of Middleware that will be applied.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Title <String>
        The title of the web page. (Default is the OpenAPI title from Enable-PodeOpenApi)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DarkMode [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the page will be rendered using a dark theme (this is not supported for all viewers).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EndpointName <String[]>
        The EndpointName of an Endpoint(s) to bind the static Route against.This parameter is normally not 
        required.
        The Endpoint is retrieved by the OpenAPI DefinitionTag
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Authentication <String>
        The name of an Authentication method which should be used as middleware on this Route.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Role <String[]>
        One or more optional Roles that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Group <String[]>
        One or more optional Groups that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Scope <String[]>
        One or more optional Scopes that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Bookmarks [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, create a new documentation bookmarks page
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -NoAdvertise [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, it is not going to state the documentation URL at the startup of the server
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Editor [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, enable the Swagger-Editor
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DefinitionTag <String>
        A string representing the unique tag for the API specification.
        This tag helps distinguish between different versions or types of API specifications within the 
        application.
        You can use this tag to reference the specific API documentation, schema, or version that your 
        function interacts with.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enable-PodeOAViewer -Type Swagger -DarkMode
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enable-PodeOAViewer -Type ReDoc -Title 'Some Title' -OpenApi 'http://some-url/openapi'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enable-PodeOAViewer -Bookmarks
    
    Adds a route that enables a viewer to display with links to any documentation tool associated with the 
    OpenApi.
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS
    https://github.com/mrin9/RapiPdf
    https://github.com/Authress-Engineering/openapi-explorer
    https://github.com/stoplightio/elements
    https://github.com/rapi-doc/RapiDoc
    https://github.com/Redocly/redoc
    https://github.com/swagger-api/swagger-ui





NAME
    Get-PodeOADefinition
    
SYNOPSIS
    Gets the OpenAPI definition.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeOADefinition [[-Format] <String>] [[-Title] <String>] [[-Version] <String>] [[-Description] 
    <String>] [[-RouteFilter] <String>] [-RestrictRoutes] [[-DefinitionTag] <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Gets the OpenAPI definition for custom use in routes, or other functions.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Format <String>
        Return the definition  in a specific format 'Json', 'Json-Compress', 'Yaml', 'HashTable'
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                HashTable
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Title <String>
        The Title of the API. (Default: the title supplied in Enable-PodeOpenApi)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Version <String>
        The Version of the API. (Default: the version supplied in Enable-PodeOpenApi)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Description <String>
        A Description of the API. (Default: the description supplied into Enable-PodeOpenApi)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    4
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -RouteFilter <String>
        An optional route filter for routes that should be included in the definition. (Default: /*)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    5
        Default value                /*
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -RestrictRoutes [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, only routes that are available on the Requests URI will be used to generate the OpenAPI 
        definition.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DefinitionTag <String>
        A string representing the unique tag for the API specification.
        This tag helps distinguish between different versions or types of API specifications within the 
        application.
        You can use this tag to reference the specific API documentation, schema, or version that your 
        function interacts with.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    6
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$defInJson = Get-PodeOADefinition -Json
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    New-PodeOAComponentSchemaProperty
    
SYNOPSIS
    Creates a OpenAPI schema reference property.
    
    
SYNTAX
    New-PodeOAComponentSchemaProperty [-ParamsList <Hashtable[]>] [-Name <String>] -Reference <String> 
    [-XmlName <String>] [-XmlNamespace <String>] [-XmlPrefix <String>] [-XmlAttribute] [-Example <Object>] 
    [-Deprecated] [-Required] [-Nullable] [-ReadOnly] [-WriteOnly] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    New-PodeOAComponentSchemaProperty [-ParamsList <Hashtable[]>] [-Name <String>] -Reference <String> 
    [-Description <String>] [-XmlName <String>] [-XmlNamespace <String>] [-XmlPrefix <String>] 
    [-XmlAttribute] [-Example <Object>] [-Deprecated] [-Required] [-Nullable] [-ReadOnly] [-WriteOnly] 
    [-XmlItemName <String>] [-XmlWrapped] -Array [-UniqueItems] [-MinItems <Int32>] [-MaxItems <Int32>] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Creates a new OpenAPI component schema reference from another OpenAPI schema.
    

PARAMETERS
    -ParamsList <Hashtable[]>
        Used to pipeline multiple properties
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the property.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Reference <String>
        An component schema name.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Description <String>
        A Description of the property.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -XmlName <String>
        By default, XML elements get the same names that fields in the API declaration have. This property 
        change the XML name of the property
        reflecting the 'xml.name' attribute in the OpenAPI specification.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -XmlNamespace <String>
        Defines a specific XML namespace for the property, corresponding to the 'xml.namespace' attribute in 
        OpenAPI.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -XmlPrefix <String>
        Sets a prefix for the XML element name, aligning with the 'xml.prefix' attribute in OpenAPI.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -XmlAttribute [<SwitchParameter>]
        Indicates whether the property should be serialized as an XML attribute, equivalent to the 
        'xml.attribute' attribute in OpenAPI.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Example <Object>
        An example of a parameter value
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Deprecated [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the schema will be treated as Deprecated where supported.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Required [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the object will be treated as Required where supported.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Nullable [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the schema will be treated as Nullable.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ReadOnly [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the schema will be included in a response but not in a request
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -WriteOnly [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the schema will be included in a request but not in a response
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -XmlItemName <String>
        Specifically for properties treated as arrays, it defines the XML name for each item in the array. 
        This parameter aligns with the 'xml.name' attribute under 'items' in OpenAPI.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -XmlWrapped [<SwitchParameter>]
        Indicates whether array items should be wrapped in an XML element, similar to the 'xml.wrapped' 
        attribute in OpenAPI.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Array [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the schema will be treated as an array of objects.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -UniqueItems [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, specify that all items in the array must be unique
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -MinItems <Int32>
        If supplied, specify minimum length of an array
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -MaxItems <Int32>
        If supplied, specify maximum length of an array
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>New-PodeOAComponentSchemaProperty -Name 'Config' -Component "MyConfigSchema"
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS
    https://swagger.io/docs/specification/basic-structure/
    https://swagger.io/docs/specification/data-models/





NAME
    Add-PodeAccess
    
SYNOPSIS
    Add an authorisation Access method.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Add-PodeAccess -Name <String> [-Description <String>] -Scheme <Hashtable> [-Match <String>] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Add-PodeAccess -Name <String> [-Description <String>] -Scheme <Hashtable> -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock> 
    [-ArgumentList <Object[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Add an authorisation Access method for use with Authentication methods, which will authorise access to 
    Routes.
    Or they can be used independant of Authentication/Routes for custom scenarios.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        A unique Name for the Access method.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Description <String>
        A short description used by OpenAPI.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Scheme <Hashtable>
        The access Scheme to use for retrieving credentials (From New-PodeAccessScheme).
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        An optional Scriptblock, which can be used to invoke custom validation logic to verify authorisation.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ArgumentList <Object[]>
        An optional array of arguments to supply to the ScriptBlock.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Match <String>
        An optional inbuilt Match method to use when verifying access to a Route, this only applies when no 
        custom Validator scriptblock is supplied. (Default: One)
        "One" will allow access if the User has at least one of the Route's access values.
        "All" will allow access only if the User has all the values.
        "None" will allow access only if the User has none of the values.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                One
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>New-PodeAccessScheme -Type Role | Add-PodeAccess -Name 'Example'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>New-PodeAccessScheme -Type Group -Path 'Metadata.Groups' | Add-PodeAccess -Name 'Example' -Match 
    All
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>New-PodeAccessScheme -Type Scope -Scriptblock { param($user) return @(Get-ExampleAccess -Username 
    $user.Username) } | Add-PodeAccess -Name 'Example'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>New-PodeAccessScheme -Custom -Path 'CustomProp' | Add-PodeAccess -Name 'Example' -ScriptBlock { 
    param($userAccess, $customAccess) return $userAccess.Country -ieq $customAccess.Country }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Add-PodeAccessCustom
    
SYNOPSIS
    Assigns Custom Access value(s) to a Route.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Add-PodeAccessCustom [-Route] <Hashtable[]> [-Name] <String> [-Value] <Object[]> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Assigns Custom Access value(s) to a Route.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Route <Hashtable[]>
        The Route to assign the Custom Access value(s).
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Access method the Custom Access value(s) are for.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Value <Object[]>
        The Custom Access Value(s)
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeRoute -Method Get -Path '/users' -ScriptBlock {} -PassThru | Add-PodeAccessCustom -Name 
    'Example' -Value @{ Country = 'UK' }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Add-PodeAccessMiddleware
    
SYNOPSIS
    Adds an access method as global middleware.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Add-PodeAccessMiddleware [-Name] <String> [-Access] <String> [[-Route] <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Adds an access method as global middleware.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Middleware.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Access <String>
        The Name of the Access method to use.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Route <String>
        A Route path for which Routes this Middleware should only be invoked against.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeAccessMiddleware -Name 'GlobalAccess' -Access AccessName
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeAccessMiddleware -Name 'GlobalAccess' -Access AccessName -Route '/api/*'

NAME
    Add-PodeAccessRule
    
SYNOPSIS
    Adds an access rule to allow or deny IP addresses. This is a legacy function, use Add-PodeLimitAccessRule 
    instead.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Add-PodeAccessRule [-Access] <String> [-Type] <String> [-Values] <String[]> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Adds an access rule to allow or deny IP addresses. This is a legacy function, use Add-PodeLimitAccessRule 
    instead.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Access <String>
        The type of access to enable.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Type <String>
        What type of request are we configuring?
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Values <String[]>
        A single, or an array of values.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeAccessRule -Access Allow -Type IP -Values '127.0.0.1'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeAccessRule -Access Deny -Type IP -Values @('192.168.1.1', '10.10.1.0/24')
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Add-PodeAuth
    
SYNOPSIS
    Adds a custom Authentication method for verifying users.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Add-PodeAuth [-Name] <String> [-Scheme] <Hashtable> [-ScriptBlock] <ScriptBlock> [[-ArgumentList] 
    <Object[]>] [[-FailureUrl] <String>] [[-FailureMessage] <String>] [[-SuccessUrl] <String>] [-Sessionless] 
    [-SuccessUseOrigin] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Adds a custom Authentication method for verifying users.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        A unique Name for the Authentication method.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Scheme <Hashtable>
        The authentication Scheme to use for retrieving credentials (From New-PodeAuthScheme).
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        The ScriptBlock defining logic that retrieves and verifys a user.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ArgumentList <Object[]>
        An array of arguments to supply to the Custom Authentication's ScriptBlock.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    4
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -FailureUrl <String>
        The URL to redirect to when authentication fails.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    5
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -FailureMessage <String>
        An override Message to throw when authentication fails.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    6
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -SuccessUrl <String>
        The URL to redirect to when authentication succeeds when logging in.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    7
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Sessionless [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, authenticated users will not be stored in sessions, and sessions will not be used.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -SuccessUseOrigin [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, successful authentication from a login page will redirect back to the originating page 
        instead of the FailureUrl.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>New-PodeAuthScheme -Form | Add-PodeAuth -Name 'Main' -ScriptBlock { /* logic */ }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Add-PodeAuthIIS
    
SYNOPSIS
    Adds the inbuilt IIS Authentication method for verifying users passed to Pode from IIS.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Add-PodeAuthIIS -Name <String> [-Groups <String[]>] [-Users <String[]>] [-FailureUrl <String>] 
    [-FailureMessage <String>] [-SuccessUrl <String>] [-ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>] [-Middleware <Object[]>] 
    [-Sessionless] [-DirectGroups] [-ADModule] [-NoLocalCheck] [-SuccessUseOrigin] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Add-PodeAuthIIS -Name <String> [-Users <String[]>] [-FailureUrl <String>] [-FailureMessage <String>] 
    [-SuccessUrl <String>] [-ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>] [-Middleware <Object[]>] [-Sessionless] [-NoGroups] 
    [-ADModule] [-NoLocalCheck] [-SuccessUseOrigin] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Adds the inbuilt IIS Authentication method for verifying users passed to Pode from IIS.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        A unique Name for the Authentication method.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Groups <String[]>
        An array of Group names to only allow access.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Users <String[]>
        An array of Usernames to only allow access.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -FailureUrl <String>
        The URL to redirect to when authentication fails.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -FailureMessage <String>
        An override Message to throw when authentication fails.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -SuccessUrl <String>
        The URL to redirect to when authentication succeeds when logging in.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        Optional ScriptBlock that is passed the found user object for further validation.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Middleware <Object[]>
        An array of ScriptBlocks for optional Middleware to run before the Scheme's scriptblock.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Sessionless [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, authenticated users will not be stored in sessions, and sessions will not be used.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -NoGroups [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, groups will not be retrieved for the user in AD.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DirectGroups [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, only a user's direct groups will be retrieved rather than all groups recursively.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ADModule [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, and on Windows, the ActiveDirectory module will be used instead.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -NoLocalCheck [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, Pode will not at attempt to retrieve local User/Group information for the authenticated 
        user.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -SuccessUseOrigin [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, successful authentication from a login page will redirect back to the originating page 
        instead of the FailureUrl.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeAuthIIS -Name 'IISAuth'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeAuthIIS -Name 'IISAuth' -Groups @('Developers')
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeAuthIIS -Name 'IISAuth' -NoGroups
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Add-PodeAuthMiddleware

    [[-OADefinitionTag] <String[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Middleware.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1

        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false

        The Name of the Authentication method to use.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false

        A Route path for which Routes this Middleware should only be invoked against.

        Position?                    3
        Default value

        Accept wildcard characters?  false

INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS

    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------

    <String>] [-Groups <String[]>] [-Users <String[]>] [-FailureUrl <String>] [-FailureMessage <String>] 
    [-SuccessUrl <String>] [-ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>] [-Sessionless] [-DirectGroups] [-OpenLDAP] 
    [-ADModule] [-SuccessUseOrigin] [-KeepCredential] [<CommonParameters>]

    -SuccessUrl <String>

        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Sessionless [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, authenticated users will not be stored in sessions, and sessions will not be used.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -NoGroups [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, groups will not be retrieved for the user in AD.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DirectGroups [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, only a user's direct groups will be retrieved rather than all groups recursively.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -OpenLDAP [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, and on Windows, OpenLDAP will be used instead (this is the default for Linux/MacOS).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ADModule [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, and on Windows, the ActiveDirectory module will be used instead.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -SuccessUseOrigin [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, successful authentication from a login page will redirect back to the originating page 
        instead of the FailureUrl.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -KeepCredential [<SwitchParameter>]
        If suplied pode will save the AD credential as a PSCredential object in $WebEvent.Auth.User.Credential
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>New-PodeAuthScheme -Form | Add-PodeAuthWindowsAd -Name 'WinAuth'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>New-PodeAuthScheme -Basic | Add-PodeAuthWindowsAd -Name 'WinAuth' -Groups @('Developers')
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>New-PodeAuthScheme -Form | Add-PodeAuthWindowsAd -Name 'WinAuth' -NoGroups
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>New-PodeAuthScheme -Form | Add-PodeAuthWindowsAd -Name 'UnixAuth' -Server 'testdomain.company.com' 
    -Domain 'testdomain'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Add-PodeAuthWindowsLocal
    
SYNOPSIS
    Adds the inbuilt Windows Local User Authentication method for verifying users.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Add-PodeAuthWindowsLocal -Name <String> -Scheme <Hashtable> [-Groups <String[]>] [-Users <String[]>] 
    [-FailureUrl <String>] [-FailureMessage <String>] [-SuccessUrl <String>] [-ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>] 
    [-Sessionless] [-SuccessUseOrigin] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Add-PodeAuthWindowsLocal -Name <String> -Scheme <Hashtable> [-Users <String[]>] [-FailureUrl <String>] 
    [-FailureMessage <String>] [-SuccessUrl <String>] [-ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>] [-Sessionless] [-NoGroups] 
    [-SuccessUseOrigin] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Adds the inbuilt Windows Local User Authentication method for verifying users.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        A unique Name for the Authentication method.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Scheme <Hashtable>
        The Scheme to use for retrieving credentials (From New-PodeAuthScheme).
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Groups <String[]>
        An array of Group names to only allow access.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Users <String[]>
        An array of Usernames to only allow access.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -FailureUrl <String>
        The URL to redirect to when authentication fails.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -FailureMessage <String>
        An override Message to throw when authentication fails.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -SuccessUrl <String>
        The URL to redirect to when authentication succeeds when logging in.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        Optional ScriptBlock that is passed the found user object for further validation.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Sessionless [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, authenticated users will not be stored in sessions, and sessions will not be used.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -NoGroups [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, groups will not be retrieved for the user.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -SuccessUseOrigin [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, successful authentication from a login page will redirect back to the originating page 
        instead of the FailureUrl.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>New-PodeAuthScheme -Form | Add-PodeAuthWindowsLocal -Name 'WinAuth'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>New-PodeAuthScheme -Basic | Add-PodeAuthWindowsLocal -Name 'WinAuth' -Groups @('Developers')
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>New-PodeAuthScheme -Form | Add-PodeAuthWindowsLocal -Name 'WinAuth' -NoGroups
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Add-PodeBodyParser
    
SYNOPSIS
    Adds a custom body parser middleware.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Add-PodeBodyParser [-ContentType] <String> [-ScriptBlock] <ScriptBlock> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Adds a custom body parser middleware script for a content-type, which will be used if a payload is sent 
    with a Request.
    

PARAMETERS
    -ContentType <String>
        The ContentType of the custom body parser.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        The ScriptBlock that will parse the body content, and return the result.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeBodyParser -ContentType 'application/json' -ScriptBlock { param($body) /* parsing logic */ 
    }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Add-PodeCacheStorage
    
SYNOPSIS
    Add a cache storage.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Add-PodeCacheStorage [-Name] <String> [-Get] <ScriptBlock> [-Set] <ScriptBlock> [-Remove] <ScriptBlock> 
    [-Test] <ScriptBlock> [-Clear] <ScriptBlock> [-Default] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Add a cache storage.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the cache storage.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Get <ScriptBlock>
        A Get ScriptBlock, to retrieve a key's value from the cache, or the value plus metadata if required. 
        Supplied parameters: Key, Metadata.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Set <ScriptBlock>
        A Set ScriptBlock, to set/create/update a key's value in the cache. Supplied parameters: Key, Value, 
        TTL.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Remove <ScriptBlock>
        A Remove ScriptBlock, to remove a key from the cache. Supplied parameters: Key.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    4
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Test <ScriptBlock>
        A Test ScriptBlock, to test if a key exists in the cache. Supplied parameters: Key.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    5
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Clear <ScriptBlock>
        A Clear ScriptBlock, to remove all keys from the cache. Use an empty ScriptBlock if not supported.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    6
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Default [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, this cache storage will be set as the default storage.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeCacheStorage -Name 'ExampleStorage' -Get {} -Set {} -Remove {} -Test {} -Clear {}
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Add-PodeEndpoint
    
SYNOPSIS
    Bind an endpoint to listen for incoming Requests.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Add-PodeEndpoint [-Address <String>] [-Port <Int32>] [-Hostname <String>] [-Protocol <String>] [-Name 
    <String>] [-RedirectTo <String>] [-Description <String>] [-Acknowledge <String>] [-SslProtocol 
    <String[]>] [-CRLFMessageEnd] [-Force] [-AllowClientCertificate] [-PassThru] [-LookupHostname] 
    [-DualMode] [-Default] [-NoAutoUpgradeWebSockets] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Add-PodeEndpoint [-Address <String>] [-Port <Int32>] [-Hostname <String>] [-Protocol <String>] 
    -Certificate <String> [-CertificatePassword <String>] [-CertificateKey <String>] [-TlsMode <String>] 
    [-Name <String>] [-RedirectTo <String>] [-Description <String>] [-Acknowledge <String>] [-SslProtocol 
    <String[]>] [-CRLFMessageEnd] [-Force] [-AllowClientCertificate] [-PassThru] [-LookupHostname] 
    [-DualMode] [-Default] [-NoAutoUpgradeWebSockets] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Add-PodeEndpoint [-Address <String>] [-Port <Int32>] [-Hostname <String>] [-Protocol <String>] 
    -CertificateThumbprint <String> [-CertificateStoreName {AddressBook | AuthRoot | CertificateAuthority | 
    Disallowed | My | Root | TrustedPeople | TrustedPublisher}] [-CertificateStoreLocation {CurrentUser | 
    LocalMachine}] [-TlsMode <String>] [-Name <String>] [-RedirectTo <String>] [-Description <String>] 
    [-Acknowledge <String>] [-SslProtocol <String[]>] [-CRLFMessageEnd] [-Force] [-AllowClientCertificate] 
    [-PassThru] [-LookupHostname] [-DualMode] [-Default] [-NoAutoUpgradeWebSockets] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Add-PodeEndpoint [-Address <String>] [-Port <Int32>] [-Hostname <String>] [-Protocol <String>] 
    -CertificateName <String> [-CertificateStoreName {AddressBook | AuthRoot | CertificateAuthority | 
    Disallowed | My | Root | TrustedPeople | TrustedPublisher}] [-CertificateStoreLocation {CurrentUser | 
    LocalMachine}] [-TlsMode <String>] [-Name <String>] [-RedirectTo <String>] [-Description <String>] 
    [-Acknowledge <String>] [-SslProtocol <String[]>] [-CRLFMessageEnd] [-Force] [-AllowClientCertificate] 
    [-PassThru] [-LookupHostname] [-DualMode] [-Default] [-NoAutoUpgradeWebSockets] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Add-PodeEndpoint [-Address <String>] [-Port <Int32>] [-Hostname <String>] [-Protocol <String>] 
    -X509Certificate <X509Certificate> [-TlsMode <String>] [-Name <String>] [-RedirectTo <String>] 
    [-Description <String>] [-Acknowledge <String>] [-SslProtocol <String[]>] [-CRLFMessageEnd] [-Force] 
    [-AllowClientCertificate] [-PassThru] [-LookupHostname] [-DualMode] [-Default] [-NoAutoUpgradeWebSockets] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Add-PodeEndpoint [-Address <String>] [-Port <Int32>] [-Hostname <String>] [-Protocol <String>] [-TlsMode 
    <String>] [-Name <String>] [-RedirectTo <String>] [-Description <String>] [-Acknowledge <String>] 
    [-SslProtocol <String[]>] [-CRLFMessageEnd] [-Force] [-SelfSigned] [-AllowClientCertificate] [-PassThru] 
    [-LookupHostname] [-DualMode] [-Default] [-NoAutoUpgradeWebSockets] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Bind an endpoint to listen for incoming Requests. The endpoints can be HTTP, HTTPS, TCP or SMTP, with the 
    option to bind certificates.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Address <String>
        The IP/Hostname of the endpoint (Default: localhost).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                localhost
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Port <Int32>
        The Port number of the endpoint.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Hostname <String>
        An optional hostname for the endpoint, specifying a hostname restricts access to just the hostname.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Protocol <String>
        The protocol of the supplied endpoint.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Certificate <String>
        The path to a certificate that can be use to enable HTTPS
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -CertificatePassword <String>
        The password for the certificate file referenced in Certificate
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -CertificateKey <String>
        A key file to be paired with a PEM certificate file referenced in Certificate
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -CertificateThumbprint <String>
        A certificate thumbprint to bind onto HTTPS endpoints (Windows).
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -CertificateName <String>
        A certificate subject name to bind onto HTTPS endpoints (Windows).
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -CertificateStoreName
        The name of a certificate store where a certificate can be found (Default: My) (Windows).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                My
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -CertificateStoreLocation
        The location of a certificate store where a certificate can be found (Default: CurrentUser) (Windows).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                CurrentUser
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -X509Certificate <X509Certificate>
        The raw X509 certificate that can be use to enable HTTPS
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -TlsMode <String>
        The TLS mode to use on secure connections, options are Implicit or Explicit (SMTP only) (Default: 
        Implicit).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                Implicit
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Name <String>
        An optional name for the endpoint, that can be used with other functions (Default: GUID).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -RedirectTo <String>
        The Name of another Endpoint to automatically generate a redirect route for all traffic.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Description <String>
        A quick description of the Endpoint - normally used in OpenAPI.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Acknowledge <String>
        An optional Acknowledge message to send to clients when they first connect, for TCP and SMTP 
        endpoints only.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -SslProtocol <String[]>
        One or more optional SSL Protocols this endpoints supports. (Default: SSL3/TLS12 - Just TLS12 on 
        MacOS).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -CRLFMessageEnd [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, TCP endpoints will expect incoming data to end with CRLF.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Force [<SwitchParameter>]
        Ignore Administrator checks for non-localhost endpoints.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
