NAME
    Add-PodeSignalRoute
    
SYNOPSIS
    Adds a Signal Route for WebSockets.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Add-PodeSignalRoute -Path <String> [-ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>] [-EndpointName <String[]>] [-ArgumentList 
    <Object[]>] [-IfExists <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Add-PodeSignalRoute -Path <String> [-EndpointName <String[]>] -FilePath <String> [-ArgumentList 
    <Object[]>] [-IfExists <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Adds a Signal Route, with path, that when called with invoke any logic.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Path <String>
        The URI path for the Signal Route.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        A ScriptBlock for the Signal Route's main logic.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EndpointName <String[]>
        The EndpointName of an Endpoint(s) this Signal Route should be bound against.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -FilePath <String>
        A literal, or relative, path to a file containing a ScriptBlock for the Signal Route's main logic.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ArgumentList <Object[]>
        An array of arguments to supply to the Signal Route's ScriptBlock.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -IfExists <String>
        Specifies what action to take when a Signal Route already exists. (Default: Default)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                Default
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Object[]
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeSignalRoute -Path '/message' -ScriptBlock { /* logic */ }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeSignalRoute -Path '/message' -ScriptBlock { /* logic */ } -ArgumentList 'arg1', 'arg2'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Add-PodeSignalRouteGroup
    
SYNOPSIS
    Adds a Signal Route Group for multiple WebSockets.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Add-PodeSignalRouteGroup [[-Path] <String>] [-Routes] <ScriptBlock> [[-EndpointName] <String[]>] 
    [[-IfExists] <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Adds a Signal Route Group for sharing values between multiple WebSockets.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Path <String>
        The URI path to use as a base for the Signal Routes, that should be prepended.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Routes <ScriptBlock>
        A ScriptBlock for adding Signal Routes.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EndpointName <String[]>
        The EndpointName of an Endpoint(s) to use for the Signal Routes.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -IfExists <String>
        Specifies what action to take when a Signal Route already exists. (Default: Default)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    4
        Default value                Default
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeSignalRouteGroup -Path '/signals' -Routes { Add-PodeSignalRoute -Path '/signal1' -Etc }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Add-PodeStaticRoute
    
SYNOPSIS
    Add a static Route for rendering static content.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Add-PodeStaticRoute [-Path] <String> [-Source] <String> [[-Middleware] <Object[]>] [[-EndpointName] 
    <String[]>] [[-ContentType] <String>] [[-TransferEncoding] <String>] [[-Defaults] <String[]>] 
    [[-ErrorContentType] <String>] [[-Authentication] <String>] [[-Access] <String>] [[-IfExists] <String>] 
    [[-Role] <String[]>] [[-Group] <String[]>] [[-Scope] <String[]>] [[-User] <String[]>] [-AllowAnon] 
    [-DownloadOnly] [-FileBrowser] [-PassThru] [-RedirectToDefault] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Add a static Route for rendering static content. You can also define default pages to display.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Path <String>
        The URI path for the static Route.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Source <String>
        The literal, or relative, path to the directory that contains the static content.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Middleware <Object[]>
        An array of ScriptBlocks for optional Middleware.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EndpointName <String[]>
        The EndpointName of an Endpoint(s) to bind the static Route against.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    4
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ContentType <String>
        The content type the static Route should use when parsing any payloads.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    5
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -TransferEncoding <String>
        The transfer encoding the static Route should use when parsing any payloads.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    6
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Defaults <String[]>
        An array of default pages to display, such as 'index.html'.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    7
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ErrorContentType <String>
        The content type of any error pages that may get returned.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    8
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Authentication <String>
        The name of an Authentication method which should be used as middleware on this Route.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    9
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Access <String>
        The name of an Access method which should be used as middleware on this Route.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    10
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -IfExists <String>
        Specifies what action to take when a Static Route already exists. (Default: Default)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    11
        Default value                Default
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Role <String[]>
        One or more optional Roles that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    12
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Group <String[]>
        One or more optional Groups that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    13
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Scope <String[]>
        One or more optional Scopes that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    14
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -User <String[]>
        One or more optional Users that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    15
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -AllowAnon [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the static route will allow anonymous access for non-authenticated users.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DownloadOnly [<SwitchParameter>]
        When supplied, all static content on this Route will be attached as downloads - rather than rendered.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -FileBrowser [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, when the path is a folder, instead of returning 404, will return A browsable content of 
        the directory.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -PassThru [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the static route created will be returned so it can be passed through a pipe.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -RedirectToDefault [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the user will be redirected to the default page if found instead of the page being 
        rendered as the folder path.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Object[]
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeStaticRoute -Path '/assets' -Source './assets'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeStaticRoute -Path '/assets' -Source './assets' -Defaults @('index.html')
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeStaticRoute -Path '/installers' -Source './exes' -DownloadOnly
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeStaticRoute -Path '/assets' -Source './assets' -Defaults @('index.html') -RedirectToDefault
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Add-PodeStaticRouteGroup
    
SYNOPSIS
    Add a Static Route Group for multiple Static Routes.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Add-PodeStaticRouteGroup [[-Path] <String>] [[-Source] <String>] [-Routes] <ScriptBlock> [[-Middleware] 
    <Object[]>] [[-EndpointName] <String[]>] [[-ContentType] <String>] [[-TransferEncoding] <String>] 
    [[-Defaults] <String[]>] [[-ErrorContentType] <String>] [[-Authentication] <String>] [[-Access] <String>] 
    [[-IfExists] <String>] [[-Role] <String[]>] [[-Group] <String[]>] [[-Scope] <String[]>] [[-User] 
    <String[]>] [-AllowAnon] [-FileBrowser] [-DownloadOnly] [-RedirectToDefault] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Add a Static Route Group for sharing values between multiple Static Routes.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Path <String>
        The URI path to use as a base for the Static Routes.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Source <String>
        A literal, or relative, base path to the directory that contains the static content, that should be 
        prepended.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Routes <ScriptBlock>
        A ScriptBlock for adding Static Routes.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Middleware <Object[]>
        An array of ScriptBlocks for optional Middleware to give each Static Route.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    4
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EndpointName <String[]>
        The EndpointName of an Endpoint(s) to use for the Static Routes.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    5
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ContentType <String>
        The content type to use for the Static Routes, when parsing any payloads.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    6
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -TransferEncoding <String>
        The transfer encoding to use for the Static Routes, when parsing any payloads.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    7
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Defaults <String[]>
        An array of default pages to display, such as 'index.html', for each Static Route.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    8
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ErrorContentType <String>
        The content type of any error pages that may get returned.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    9
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Authentication <String>
        The name of an Authentication method which should be used as middleware on the Static Routes.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    10
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Access <String>
        The name of an Access method which should be used as middleware on this Route.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    11
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -IfExists <String>
        Specifies what action to take when a Static Route already exists. (Default: Default)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    12
        Default value                Default
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Role <String[]>
        One or more optional Roles that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    13
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Group <String[]>
        One or more optional Groups that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    14
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Scope <String[]>
        One or more optional Scopes that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    15
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -User <String[]>
        One or more optional Users that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    16
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -AllowAnon [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the Static Routes will allow anonymous access for non-authenticated users.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -FileBrowser [<SwitchParameter>]
        When supplied, If the path is a folder, instead of returning 404, will return A browsable content of 
        the directory.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DownloadOnly [<SwitchParameter>]
        When supplied, all static content on the Routes will be attached as downloads - rather than rendered.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -RedirectToDefault [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the user will be redirected to the default page if found instead of the page being 
        rendered as the folder path.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeStaticRouteGroup -Path '/static' -Routes { Add-PodeStaticRoute -Path '/images' -Etc }

NAME
    Add-PodeTask
    
SYNOPSIS
    Adds a new Task.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Add-PodeTask -Name <String> -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock> [-ArgumentList <Hashtable>] [-Timeout <Int32>] 
    [-TimeoutFrom <String>] [-MaxRetries <Int32>] [-RetryDelay <Int32>] [-AutoRetry] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Add-PodeTask -Name <String> -FilePath <String> [-ArgumentList <Hashtable>] [-Timeout <Int32>] 
    [-TimeoutFrom <String>] [-MaxRetries <Int32>] [-RetryDelay <Int32>] [-AutoRetry] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Adds a new Task, which can be asynchronously or synchronously invoked.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Task.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        The script for the Task.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -FilePath <String>
        A literal, or relative, path to a file containing a ScriptBlock for the Task's logic.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ArgumentList <Hashtable>
        A hashtable of arguments to supply to the Task's ScriptBlock.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Timeout <Int32>
        A Timeout, in seconds, to abort running the Task process. (Default: -1 [never timeout])
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                -1
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -TimeoutFrom <String>
        Where to start the Timeout from, either 'Create', 'Start'. (Default: 'Create')
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                Create
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -MaxRetries <Int32>
        The maximum number of retries to attempt if the Task fails. (Default: 0)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -RetryDelay <Int32>
        The delay, in minutes, between automatically retrying failed task processes. (Default: 0)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -AutoRetry [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the Task will automatically retry processes if they fail.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeTask -Name 'Example1' -ScriptBlock { Invoke-SomeLogic }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeTask -Name 'Example1' -ScriptBlock { return Get-SomeObject }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeTask -Name 'Example1' -ScriptBlock { return Get-SomeObject } -MaxRetries 3 -RetryDelay 5 
    -AutoRetry
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Add-PodeTimer
    
SYNOPSIS
    Adds a new Timer with logic to periodically invoke.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Add-PodeTimer -Name <String> -Interval <Int32> -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock> [-Limit <Int32>] [-Skip 
    <Int32>] [-ArgumentList <Object[]>] [-OnStart] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Add-PodeTimer -Name <String> -Interval <Int32> [-Limit <Int32>] [-Skip <Int32>] -FilePath <String> 
    [-ArgumentList <Object[]>] [-OnStart] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Adds a new Timer with logic to periodically invoke, with options to only run a specific number of times.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Timer.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Interval <Int32>
        The number of seconds to periodically invoke the Timer's ScriptBlock.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        The script for the Timer.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Limit <Int32>
        The number of times the Timer should be invoked before being removed. (If 0, it will run indefinitely)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Skip <Int32>
        The number of "invokes" to skip before the Timer actually runs.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -FilePath <String>
        A literal, or relative, path to a file containing a ScriptBlock for the Timer's logic.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ArgumentList <Object[]>
        An array of arguments to supply to the Timer's ScriptBlock.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -OnStart [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the timer will trigger when the server starts.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeTimer -Name 'Hello' -Interval 10 -ScriptBlock { 'Hello, world!' | Out-Default }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeTimer -Name 'RunOnce' -Interval 1 -Limit 1 -ScriptBlock { /* logic */ }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeTimer -Name 'RunAfter60secs' -Interval 10 -Skip 6 -ScriptBlock { /* logic */ }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeTimer -Name 'Args' -Interval 2 -ScriptBlock { /* logic */ } -ArgumentList 'arg1', 'arg2'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Add-PodeVerb
    
SYNOPSIS
    Adds a Verb for a TCP data.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Add-PodeVerb -Verb <String> [-ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>] [-ArgumentList <Object[]>] [-EndpointName 
    <String[]>] [-UpgradeToSsl] [-Close] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Add-PodeVerb -Verb <String> -FilePath <String> [-ArgumentList <Object[]>] [-EndpointName <String[]>] 
    [-UpgradeToSsl] [-Close] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Adds a Verb for a TCP data.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Verb <String>
        The Verb for the Verb.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        A ScriptBlock for the Verb's main logic.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -FilePath <String>
        A literal, or relative, path to a file containing a ScriptBlock for the Verb's main logic.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ArgumentList <Object[]>
        An array of arguments to supply to the Verb's ScriptBlock.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EndpointName <String[]>
        The EndpointName of an Endpoint(s) this Verb should be bound against.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -UpgradeToSsl [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the Verb will auto-upgrade the connection to use SSL.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Close [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the Verb will auto-close the connection.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeVerb -Verb 'Hello' -ScriptBlock { /* logic */ }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeVerb -Verb 'Hello' -ScriptBlock { /* logic */ } -ArgumentList 'arg1', 'arg2'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeVerb -Verb 'Quit' -Close
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeVerb -Verb 'StartTls' -UpgradeToSsl
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Clear-PodeTasks
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

NAME
    Close-PodeDisposable
    
SYNOPSIS
    Dispose and close streams, tokens, and other Disposables.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Close-PodeDisposable [[-Disposable] <IDisposable>] [-Close] [-CheckNetwork] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Dispose and close streams, tokens, and other Disposables.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Disposable <IDisposable>
        The Disposable object to dispose and close.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Close [<SwitchParameter>]
        Should the Disposable also be closed, as well as disposed?
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -CheckNetwork [<SwitchParameter>]
        If an error is thrown, check the reason - if it's network related ignore the error.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Close-PodeDisposable -Disposable $stream -Close
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Close-PodeServer
    
SYNOPSIS
    Closes the Pode server.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Close-PodeServer [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Closes the Pode server.
    

PARAMETERS
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Close-PodeServer
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Close-PodeSignalConnection
    
SYNOPSIS
    Close one or more Signal connections.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Close-PodeSignalConnection [-Name] <String> [[-Group] <String[]>] [[-ClientId] <String[]>] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Close one or more Signal connections. Either all connections for a Signal connection Name, or specific 
    ClientIds for a Name.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Signal connection which has the ClientIds for the connections to close. If supplied 
        on its own, all connections will be closed.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Group <String[]>
        An optional array of 1 or more Signal connection Groups, that are for the Signal connection Name. If 
        supplied without any ClientIds, then all connections for the Group(s) will be closed.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ClientId <String[]>
        An optional array of 1 or more Signal connection ClientIds, that are for the Signal connection Name.
        If not supplied, every Signal connection for the supplied Name will be closed.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Close-PodeSignalConnection -Name 'Metrics'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Close-PodeSignalConnection -Name 'Metrics' -Group 'admins'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Close-PodeSignalConnection -Name 'Metrics' -ClientId @('my-client-id', 'my-other'id')
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Close-PodeSseConnection
    
SYNOPSIS
    Close one or more SSE connections.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Close-PodeSseConnection [-Name] <String> [[-Group] <String[]>] [[-ClientId] <String[]>] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Close one or more SSE connections. Either all connections for an SSE connection Name, or specific 
    ClientIds for a Name.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the SSE connection which has the ClientIds for the connections to close. If supplied on 
        its own, all connections will be closed.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Group <String[]>
        An optional array of 1 or more SSE connection Groups, that are for the SSE connection Name. If 
        supplied without any ClientIds, then all connections for the Group(s) will be closed.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ClientId <String[]>
        An optional array of 1 or more SSE connection ClientIds, that are for the SSE connection Name.
        If not supplied, every SSE connection for the supplied Name will be closed.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Close-PodeSseConnection -Name 'Actions'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Close-PodeSseConnection -Name 'Actions' -Group 'admins'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Close-PodeSseConnection -Name 'Actions' -ClientId @('my-client-id', 'my-other'id')
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Close-PodeTask
    
SYNOPSIS
    Close and dispose of a Task.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Close-PodeTask [-Process] <Hashtable> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Close and dispose of a Task, even if still running.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Process <Hashtable>
        The Task to be closed.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Invoke-PodeTask -Name 'Example1' | Close-PodeTask

NAME
    Close-PodeTcpClient
    
SYNOPSIS
    Close an open TCP client connection
    
    
SYNTAX
    Close-PodeTcpClient [<CommonParameters>]
