NAME
    Connect-PodeWebSocket
    
SYNOPSIS
    Connect to an external WebSocket.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Connect-PodeWebSocket -Name <String> -Url <String> [-ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>] [-ContentType <String>] 
    [-ArgumentList <Object[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Connect-PodeWebSocket -Name <String> -Url <String> -FilePath <String> [-ContentType <String>] 
    [-ArgumentList <Object[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Connect to an external WebSocket.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the WebSocket connection.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Url <String>
        The URL of the WebSocket. Should start with either ws:// or wss://.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        The ScriptBlock to invoke for processing received messages from the WebSocket. The ScriptBlock will 
        have access to a $WsEvent variable with details of the received message.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -FilePath <String>
        A literal, or relative, path to a file containing a ScriptBlock for the WebSocket's logic.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ContentType <String>
        An optional ContentType for parsing/converting received/sent messages. (default: application/json)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                application/json
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ArgumentList <Object[]>
        AN optional array of extra arguments, that will be passed to the ScriptBlock.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Connect-PodeWebSocket -Name 'Example' -Url 'ws://example.com/some/socket' -ScriptBlock { ... }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Connect-PodeWebSocket -Name 'Example' -Url 'ws://example.com/some/socket' -ScriptBlock { 
    param($arg1, $arg2) ... } -ArgumentList 'arg1', 'arg2'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Connect-PodeWebSocket -Name 'Example' -Url 'ws://example.com/some/socket' -FilePath 
    './some/path/file.ps1'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Connect-PodeWebSocket -Name 'Example' -Url 'ws://example.com/some/socket' -ScriptBlock { ... } 
    -ContentType 'text/xml'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    ConvertFrom-PodeJwt
    
SYNOPSIS
    Convert and return the payload of a JWT token.
    
    
SYNTAX
    ConvertFrom-PodeJwt -Token <String> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    ConvertFrom-PodeJwt -Token <String> [-Secret <Object>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    ConvertFrom-PodeJwt -Token <String> [-IgnoreSignature] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Convert and return the payload of a JWT token, verifying the signature by default with support to ignore 
    the signature.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Token <String>
        The JWT token.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Secret <Object>
        The Secret, as a string or byte[], to verify the token's signature.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -IgnoreSignature [<SwitchParameter>]
        Skip signature verification, and return the decoded payload.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Management.Automation.PSObject
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>ConvertFrom-PodeJwt -Token "eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJoczI1NiJ9.eyJleHAiOjE2MjI1NTMyMTQsIm5hbWUiOi
    JKb2huIERvZSIsInN1YiI6IjEyMyJ9.LP-O8OKwix91a-SZwVK35gEClLZQmsORbW0un2Z4RkY"
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    ConvertFrom-PodeOIDCDiscovery
    
SYNOPSIS
    Builds an OAuth2 scheme using an OpenID Connect Discovery URL.
    
    
SYNTAX
    ConvertFrom-PodeOIDCDiscovery [-Url] <String> [[-Scope] <String[]>] [-ClientId] <String> [[-ClientSecret] 
    <String>] [[-RedirectUrl] <String>] [[-InnerScheme] <Hashtable>] [[-Middleware] <Object[]>] [-UsePKCE] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Builds an OAuth2 scheme using an OpenID Connect Discovery URL.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Url <String>
        The OpenID Connect Discovery URL, this must end with '/.well-known/openid-configuration' (if missing, 
        it will be automatically appended).
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Scope <String[]>
        A list of optional Scopes to use during the OAuth2 request. (Default: the supported list returned)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ClientId <String>
        The Client ID from registering a new app.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ClientSecret <String>
        The Client Secret from registering a new app (this is optional when using PKCE).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    4
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -RedirectUrl <String>
        An optional OAuth2 Redirect URL (Default: <host>/oauth2/callback)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    5
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -InnerScheme <Hashtable>
        An optional authentication Scheme (from New-PodeAuthScheme) that will be called prior to this Scheme.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    6
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Middleware <Object[]>
        An array of ScriptBlocks for optional Middleware to run before the Scheme's scriptblock.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    7
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -UsePKCE [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, OAuth2 authentication will use PKCE code verifiers.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>ConvertFrom-PodeOIDCDiscovery -Url 'https://accounts.google.com/.well-known/openid-configuration' 
    -ClientId some_id -UsePKCE
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>ConvertFrom-PodeOIDCDiscovery -Url 'https://accounts.google.com' -ClientId some_id -UsePKCE
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    ConvertFrom-PodeXml
    
SYNOPSIS
    Converts an XML node to a PowerShell hashtable.
    
    
SYNTAX
    ConvertFrom-PodeXml [-node] <XmlNode> [-Prefix <String>] [-ShowDocElement] [-KeepAttributes] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    The ConvertFrom-PodeXml function converts an XML node, including all its child nodes and attributes, into 
    an ordered hashtable. This is useful for manipulating XML data in a more PowerShell-centric way.
    

PARAMETERS
    -node <XmlNode>
        The XML node to convert. This parameter takes an XML node and processes it, along with its child 
        nodes and attributes.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Prefix <String>
        A string prefix used to indicate an attribute. Default is an empty string.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ShowDocElement [<SwitchParameter>]
        Indicates whether to show the document element. Default is false.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -KeepAttributes [<SwitchParameter>]
        If set, the function keeps the attributes of the XML nodes in the resulting hashtable.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    System.Xml.XmlNode
    You can pipe a XmlNode to ConvertFrom-PodeXml.
    
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Collections.Hashtable
    Outputs an ordered hashtable representing the XML node structure.
    
    
NOTES
    
    
        This cmdlet is useful for transforming XML data into a structure that's easier to manipulate in 
        PowerShell scripts.
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$node = [xml](Get-Content 'path\to\file.xml').DocumentElement
    
    ConvertFrom-PodeXml -node $node
    
    Converts the XML document's root node to a hashtable.
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Convert-PodeScopedVariable
    
SYNOPSIS
    Converts a Scoped Variable within a given ScriptBlock.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Convert-PodeScopedVariable [-Name] <String> [[-ScriptBlock] <ScriptBlock>] [[-PSSession] <SessionState>] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Converts a Scoped Variable within a given ScriptBlock, and returns the updated ScriptBlock back, 
    including any
    other values that will need to be supplied as parameters to the ScriptBlock first.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Scoped Variable to convert. (ie: Session, Using, or a Name from 
        Add-PodeScopedVariable)
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        The ScriptBlock to be converted.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -PSSession <SessionState>
        An optional SessionState object, used to retrieve using-variable values or other values where scope 
        is required.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Management.Automation.ScriptBlock
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$ScriptBlock = Convert-PodeScopedVariable -Name State -ScriptBlock $ScriptBlock
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$ScriptBlock, $otherResults = Convert-PodeScopedVariable -Name Using -ScriptBlock $ScriptBlock
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Convert-PodeScopedVariables
    
SYNOPSIS
    Converts Scoped Variables within a given ScriptBlock.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Convert-PodeScopedVariables [[-ScriptBlock] <ScriptBlock>] [[-PSSession] <SessionState>] [[-Exclude] 
    <String[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Converts Scoped Variables within a given ScriptBlock, and returns the updated ScriptBlock back, including 
    any
    using-variable values that will need to be supplied as parameters to the ScriptBlock first.
    

PARAMETERS
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        The ScriptBlock to be converted.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -PSSession <SessionState>
        An optional SessionState object, used to retrieve using-variable values.
        If not supplied, using-variable values will not be converted.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Exclude <String[]>
        An optional array of one or more Scoped Variable Names to Exclude from converting. (ie: Session, 
        Using, or a Name from Add-PodeScopedVariable)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Object[]
    
    System.Management.Automation.ScriptBlock
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$ScriptBlock, $usingVars = Convert-PodeScopedVariables -ScriptBlock $ScriptBlock -PSSession 
    $PSCmdlet.SessionState
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$ScriptBlock = Convert-PodeScopedVariables -ScriptBlock $ScriptBlock -Exclude Session, Using
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    ConvertTo-PodeJwt
    
SYNOPSIS
    Convert a Header/Payload into a JWT.
    
    
SYNTAX
    ConvertTo-PodeJwt [-Header] <Hashtable> [-Payload] <Hashtable> [[-Secret] <Object>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Convert a Header/Payload hashtable into a JWT, with the option to sign it.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Header <Hashtable>
        A Hashtable containing the Header information for the JWT.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Payload <Hashtable>
        A Hashtable containing the Payload information for the JWT.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Secret <Object>
        An Optional Secret for signing the JWT, should be a string or byte[]. This is mandatory if the Header 
        algorithm isn't "none".
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.String
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>ConvertTo-PodeJwt -Header @{ alg = 'none' } -Payload @{ sub = '123'; name = 'John' }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>ConvertTo-PodeJwt -Header @{ alg = 'hs256' } -Payload @{ sub = '123'; name = 'John' } -Secret 'abc'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    ConvertTo-PodeOAParameter
    
SYNOPSIS
    Converts an OpenAPI property into a Request Parameter.
    
    
SYNTAX
    ConvertTo-PodeOAParameter -Reference <String> [-DefinitionTag <String[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    ConvertTo-PodeOAParameter -In <String> [-Property] <Hashtable> [-Name <String>] -ContentType <String> 
    [-Description <String>] [-Required] [-Example <Object>] [-Examples <OrderedDictionary>] [-Deprecated] 
    [-DefinitionTag <String[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    ConvertTo-PodeOAParameter -In <String> [-Name <String>] -Schema <String> -ContentType <String> 
    [-Description <String>] [-Required] [-AllowEmptyValue] [-Example <Object>] [-Examples 
    <OrderedDictionary>] [-Deprecated] [-DefinitionTag <String[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    ConvertTo-PodeOAParameter -In <String> [-Property] <Hashtable> [-Name <String>] [-Description <String>] 
    [-Explode] [-Required] [-AllowEmptyValue] [-AllowReserved] [-Example <Object>] [-Examples 
    <OrderedDictionary>] [-Style <String>] [-Deprecated] [-DefinitionTag <String[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    ConvertTo-PodeOAParameter -In <String> [-Name <String>] -Schema <String> [-Description <String>] 
    [-Explode] [-Required] [-AllowEmptyValue] [-AllowReserved] [-Example <Object>] [-Examples 
    <OrderedDictionary>] [-Style <String>] [-Deprecated] [-DefinitionTag <String[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Converts an OpenAPI property (such as from New-PodeOAIntProperty) into a Request Parameter.
    

PARAMETERS
    -In <String>
        Where in the Request can the parameter be found?
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Property <Hashtable>
        The Property that need converting (such as from New-PodeOAIntProperty).
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Reference <String>
        The name of an existing component parameter to be reused.
        Alias: ComponentParameter
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Name <String>
        Assign a name to the parameter
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Schema <String>
        The component schema to use.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ContentType <String>
        The content-types to be use with  component schema
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Description <String>
        A Description of the property.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Explode [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, controls how arrays are serialized in query parameters
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Required [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the object will be treated as Required where supported.(Applicable only to ContentSchema)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -AllowEmptyValue [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, allow the parameter to be empty
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -AllowReserved [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, determines whether the parameter value SHOULD allow reserved characters, as defined by 
        RFC3986 :/?#[]@!$&'()*+,;= to be included without percent-encoding.
        This property only applies to parameters with an in value of query. The default value is false.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Example <Object>
        Example of the parameter's potential value. The example SHOULD match the specified schema and 
        encoding properties if present.
        The Example parameter is mutually exclusive of the Examples parameter.
        Furthermore, if referencing a Schema  that contains an example, the Example value SHALL _override_ 
        the example provided by the schema.
        To represent examples of media types that cannot naturally be represented in JSON or YAML, a string 
        value can contain the example with escaping where necessary.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Examples <OrderedDictionary>
        Examples of the parameter's potential value. Each example SHOULD contain a value in the correct 
        format as specified in the parameter encoding.
        The Examples parameter is mutually exclusive of the Example parameter.
        Furthermore, if referencing a Schema that contains an example, the Examples value SHALL _override_ 
        the example provided by the schema.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Style <String>
        If supplied, defines how multiple values are delimited. Possible styles depend on the parameter 
        location: path, query, header or cookie.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Deprecated [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, specifies that a parameter is deprecated and SHOULD be transitioned out of usage. 
        Default value is false.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DefinitionTag <String[]>
        An Array of strings representing the unique tag for the API specification.
        This tag helps distinguish between different versions or types of API specifications within the 
        application.
        You can use this tag to reference the specific API documentation, schema, or version that your 
        function interacts with.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>New-PodeOAIntProperty -Name 'userId' | ConvertTo-PodeOAParameter -In Query
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>ConvertTo-PodeOAParameter -Reference 'UserIdParam'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>ConvertTo-PodeOAParameter  -In Header -ContentSchemas @{ 'application/json' = 'UserIdSchema' }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    ConvertTo-PodeRoute
    
SYNOPSIS
    Takes an array of Commands, or a Module, and converts them into Routes.
    
    
SYNTAX
    ConvertTo-PodeRoute [[-Commands] <String[]>] [-Module <String>] [-Method <String>] [-Path <String>] 
    [-Middleware <Object[]>] [-Authentication <String>] [-Access <String>] [-Role <String[]>] [-Group 
    <String[]>] [-Scope <String[]>] [-User <String[]>] [-AllowAnon] [-NoVerb] [-NoOpenApi] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Takes an array of Commands (Functions/Aliases), or a Module, and generates appropriate Routes for the 
    commands.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Commands <String[]>
        An array of Commands to convert - if a Module is supplied, these Commands must be present within that 
        Module.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Module <String>
        A Module whose exported commands will be converted.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Method <String>
        An override HTTP method to use when generating the Routes. If not supplied, Pode will make a best 
        guess based on the Command's Verb.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Path <String>
        An optional Path for the Route, to prepend before the Command Name and Module.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                /
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Middleware <Object[]>
        Like normal Routes, an array of Middleware that will be applied to all generated Routes.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Authentication <String>
        The name of an Authentication method which should be used as middleware on this Route.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Access <String>
        The name of an Access method which should be used as middleware on this Route.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Role <String[]>
        One or more optional Roles that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Group <String[]>
        One or more optional Groups that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Scope <String[]>
        One or more optional Scopes that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -User <String[]>
        One or more optional Users that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -AllowAnon [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the Route will allow anonymous access for non-authenticated users.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -NoVerb [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the Command's Verb will not be included in the Route's path.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -NoOpenApi [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, no OpenAPI definitions will be generated for the routes created.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>ConvertTo-PodeRoute -Commands @('Get-ChildItem', 'Get-Host', 'Invoke-Expression') -Middleware { 
    ... }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>ConvertTo-PodeRoute -Commands @('Get-ChildItem', 'Get-Host', 'Invoke-Expression') -Authentication 
    AuthName
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>ConvertTo-PodeRoute -Module Pester -Path '/api'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>ConvertTo-PodeRoute -Commands @('Invoke-Pester') -Module Pester
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    ConvertTo-PodeSignalConnection
    
SYNOPSIS
    Converts the current HTTP request to a Route to be a Signal (WebSocket) connection.
    
    
SYNTAX
    ConvertTo-PodeSignalConnection [-Name] <String> [[-Group] <String>] [[-Scope] <String>] [[-ClientId] 
    <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Converts the current HTTP request to a Route to be an Signal (WebSocket) connection, by sending the 
    required headers back to the client.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Signal connection, which ClientIds will be stored under.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Group <String>
        An optional Group for the Signal connection, to enable broadcasting events to all connections for an 
        Signal connection name in a Group.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Scope <String>
        The Scope of the Signal connection, either Default, Local or Global (Default: Default).
        - If the Scope is Default, then it will be Global unless the default has been updated via 
        Set-PodeSignalDefaultScope.
        - If the Scope is Local, then the Signal connection will only be opened for the duration of the 
        request to a Route that configured it.
        - If the Scope is Global, then the Signal connection will be cached internally so events can be sent 
        to the connection from Tasks, Timers, and other Routes, etc.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                Default
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ClientId <String>
        An optional ClientId to use for the Signal connection, this value will be signed if signing is 
        enabled (Default: GUID).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    4
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>ConvertTo-PodeSignalConnection -Name 'Metrics'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>ConvertTo-PodeSignalConnection -Name 'Metrics' -Scope Local
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>ConvertTo-PodeSignalConnection -Name 'Metrics' -Group 'admins'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>ConvertTo-PodeSignalConnection -Name 'Metrics' -ClientId 'my-client-id'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    ConvertTo-PodeSseConnection
    
SYNOPSIS
    Converts the current HTTP request to a Route to be an SSE connection.
    
    
SYNTAX
    ConvertTo-PodeSseConnection [-Name] <String> [[-Group] <String>] [[-Scope] <String>] [[-RetryDuration] 
    <Int32>] [[-ClientId] <String>] [-AllowAllOrigins] [-Force] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Converts the current HTTP request to a Route to be an SSE connection, by sending the required headers 
    back to the client.
    The connection can only be configured if the request's Accept header is "text/event-stream", unless 
    Forced.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the SSE connection, which ClientIds will be stored under.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Group <String>
        An optional Group for this SSE connection, to enable broadcasting events to all connections for an 
        SSE connection name in a Group.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Scope <String>
        The Scope of the SSE connection, either Default, Local or Global (Default: Default).
        - If the Scope is Default, then it will be Global unless the default has been updated via 
        Set-PodeSseDefaultScope.
        - If the Scope is Local, then the SSE connection will only be opened for the duration of the request 
        to a Route that configured it.
        - If the Scope is Global, then the SSE connection will be cached internally so events can be sent to 
        the connection from Tasks, Timers, and other Routes, etc.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                Default
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -RetryDuration <Int32>
        An optional RetryDuration, in milliseconds, for the period of time a browser should wait before 
        reattempting a connection if lost (Default: 0).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    4
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ClientId <String>
        An optional ClientId to use for the SSE connection, this value will be signed if signing is enabled 
        (Default: GUID).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    5
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -AllowAllOrigins [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, then Access-Control-Allow-Origin will be set to * on the response.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Force [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the Accept header of the request will be ignored; attempting to configure an SSE 
        connection even if the header isn't "text/event-stream".
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>ConvertTo-PodeSseConnection -Name 'Actions'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>ConvertTo-PodeSseConnection -Name 'Actions' -Scope Local
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>ConvertTo-PodeSseConnection -Name 'Actions' -Group 'admins'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>ConvertTo-PodeSseConnection -Name 'Actions' -AllowAllOrigins
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 5 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>ConvertTo-PodeSseConnection -Name 'Actions' -ClientId 'my-client-id'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Disable-PodeErrorLogging
    
SYNOPSIS
    Disables Error Logging.

    Disable-PodeRequestLogging [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION

PARAMETERS

        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see

OUTPUTS

    PS C:\>Disable-PodeRequestLogging

NAME
    Disable-PodeServer
    
SYNOPSIS
    Blocks new incoming requests by adding middleware that returns a 503 Service Unavailable status when the 
    Pode Watchdog client is active.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Disable-PodeServer [[-RetryAfter] <Int32>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    This function integrates middleware into the Pode server, preventing new incoming requests while the Pode 
    Watchdog client is active.
    All requests receive a 503 Service Unavailable response, including a 'Retry-After' header that specifies 
    when the service will become available.
    

PARAMETERS
    -RetryAfter <Int32>
        Specifies the time in seconds clients should wait before retrying their requests. Default is 3600 
        seconds (1 hour).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                3600
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    
RELATED LINKS

    Disable-PodeSignalSigning
    
SYNOPSIS

SYNTAX
    Disable-PodeSignalSigning [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Disable the signing of Signal connection ClientIds.
    

PARAMETERS

        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Disable-PodeSignalSigning
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

    Disable-PodeSseSigning

        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216).
