NAME
    Disconnect-PodeWebSocket
    
SYNOPSIS
    Disconnect from a WebSocket connection.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Disconnect-PodeWebSocket [[-Name] <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Disconnect from a WebSocket connection. These connections can be reconnected later using 
    Reset-PodeWebSocket
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the WebSocket connection (optional if in the scope where $WsEvent is available).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Disconnect-PodeWebSocket -Name 'Example'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Dismount-PodeSecret
    
SYNOPSIS
    Dismount a previously mounted Secret.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Dismount-PodeSecret [-Name] <String> [-Remove] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Dismount a previously mounted Secret.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The friendly Name of the Secret.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Remove [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the Secret will also be removed from the Secret Vault as well.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Dismount-PodeSecret -Name 'SecretName'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Dismount-PodeSecret -Name 'SecretName' -Remove
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Edit-PodeSchedule
    
SYNOPSIS
    Edits an existing Schedule.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Edit-PodeSchedule [-Name] <String> [[-Cron] <String[]>] [[-ScriptBlock] <ScriptBlock>] [[-ArgumentList] 
    <Hashtable>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Edits an existing Schedule's properties, such an cron expressions or scriptblock.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Schedule.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Cron <String[]>
        Any new Cron Expressions for the Schedule.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        The new ScriptBlock for the Schedule.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ArgumentList <Hashtable>
        Any new Arguments for the Schedule.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    4
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Edit-PodeSchedule -Name 'Hello' -Cron '@minutely'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Edit-PodeSchedule -Name 'Hello' -Cron @('@hourly', '0 0 * * TUE')
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Edit-PodeTask
    
SYNOPSIS
    Edits an existing Task.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Edit-PodeTask [-Name] <String> [-ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>] [-ArgumentList <Hashtable>] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Edits an existing Task's properties, such as scriptblock.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Task.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        The new ScriptBlock for the Task.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ArgumentList <Hashtable>
        Any new Arguments for the Task.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Edit-PodeTask -Name 'Example1' -ScriptBlock { Invoke-SomeNewLogic }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Edit-PodeTimer
    
SYNOPSIS
    Edits an existing Timer.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Edit-PodeTimer [-Name] <String> [-Interval <Int32>] [-ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>] [-ArgumentList 
    <Object[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Edits an existing Timer's properties, such as interval or scriptblock.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Timer.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Interval <Int32>
        The new Interval for the Timer in seconds.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        The new ScriptBlock for the Timer.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ArgumentList <Object[]>
        Any new Arguments for the Timer.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Edit-PodeTimer -Name 'Hello' -Interval 10
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Enable-PodeCsrfMiddleware
    
SYNOPSIS
    Enables Middleware for verifying CSRF tokens on Requests.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Enable-PodeCsrfMiddleware [-IgnoreMethods <String[]>] [-Secret <String>] [-UseCookies] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Enables Middleware for verifying CSRF tokens on Requests, with configurable HTTP methods to ignore 
    verification.
    

PARAMETERS
    -IgnoreMethods <String[]>
        An array of HTTP methods to ignore CSRF verification.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                @('Get', 'Head', 'Options', 'Trace')
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Secret <String>
        A secret to use when signing cookies - for when using CSRF with cookies.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -UseCookies [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, CSRF will used cookies rather than sessions.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enable-PodeCsrfMiddleware -IgnoreMethods @('Get', 'Trace')
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enable-PodeCsrfMiddleware -Secret 'some-secret' -UseCookies
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Enable-PodeErrorLogging
    
SYNOPSIS
    Enables Error Logging using a supplied output method.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Enable-PodeErrorLogging [-Method] <Hashtable> [[-Levels] <String[]>] [-Raw] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Enables Error Logging using a supplied output method.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Method <Hashtable>
        The Method to use for output the log entry (From New-PodeLoggingMethod).
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Levels <String[]>
        The Levels of errors that should be logged (default is Error).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                @('Error')
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Raw [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the log item returned will be the raw Error item as a hashtable and not a string (for 
        Custom methods).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>New-PodeLoggingMethod -Terminal | Enable-PodeErrorLogging
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Enable-PodeOAViewer
    
SYNOPSIS
    Adds a route that enables a viewer to display OpenAPI docs, such as Swagger, ReDoc, RapiDoc, StopLight, 
    Explorer, RapiPdf or Bookmarks.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Enable-PodeOAViewer -Type <String> [-Path <String>] [-OpenApiUrl <String>] [-Middleware <Object[]>] 
    [-Title <String>] [-DarkMode] [-EndpointName <String[]>] [-Authentication <String>] [-Role <String[]>] 
    [-Group <String[]>] [-Scope <String[]>] [-DefinitionTag <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Enable-PodeOAViewer [-Path <String>] [-OpenApiUrl <String>] [-Middleware <Object[]>] [-Title <String>] 
    [-DarkMode] [-EndpointName <String[]>] [-Authentication <String>] [-Role <String[]>] [-Group <String[]>] 
    [-Scope <String[]>] -Bookmarks [-NoAdvertise] [-DefinitionTag <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Enable-PodeOAViewer [-Path <String>] [-OpenApiUrl <String>] [-Middleware <Object[]>] [-Title <String>] 
    [-DarkMode] [-EndpointName <String[]>] [-Authentication <String>] [-Role <String[]>] [-Group <String[]>] 
    [-Scope <String[]>] -Editor [-DefinitionTag <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Adds a route that enables a viewer to display OpenAPI docs, such as Swagger, ReDoc, RapiDoc, StopLight, 
    Explorer, RapiPdf  or Bookmarks.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Type <String>
        The Type of OpenAPI viewer to use.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Path <String>
        The route Path where the docs can be accessed. (Default: "/$Type")
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -OpenApiUrl <String>
        The URL where the OpenAPI definition can be retrieved. (Default is the OpenAPI path from 
        Enable-PodeOpenApi)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Middleware <Object[]>
        Like normal Routes, an array of Middleware that will be applied.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Title <String>
        The title of the web page. (Default is the OpenAPI title from Enable-PodeOpenApi)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DarkMode [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the page will be rendered using a dark theme (this is not supported for all viewers).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EndpointName <String[]>
        The EndpointName of an Endpoint(s) to bind the static Route against.This parameter is normally not 
        required.
        The Endpoint is retrieved by the OpenAPI DefinitionTag
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Authentication <String>
        The name of an Authentication method which should be used as middleware on this Route.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Role <String[]>
        One or more optional Roles that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Group <String[]>
        One or more optional Groups that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Scope <String[]>
        One or more optional Scopes that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Bookmarks [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, create a new documentation bookmarks page
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -NoAdvertise [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, it is not going to state the documentation URL at the startup of the server
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Editor [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, enable the Swagger-Editor
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DefinitionTag <String>
        A string representing the unique tag for the API specification.
        This tag helps distinguish between different versions or types of API specifications within the 
        application.
        You can use this tag to reference the specific API documentation, schema, or version that your 
        function interacts with.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enable-PodeOAViewer -Type Swagger -DarkMode
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enable-PodeOAViewer -Type ReDoc -Title 'Some Title' -OpenApi 'http://some-url/openapi'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enable-PodeOAViewer -Bookmarks
    
    Adds a route that enables a viewer to display with links to any documentation tool associated with the 
    OpenApi.
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS
    https://github.com/mrin9/RapiPdf
    https://github.com/Authress-Engineering/openapi-explorer
    https://github.com/stoplightio/elements
    https://github.com/rapi-doc/RapiDoc
    https://github.com/Redocly/redoc
    https://github.com/swagger-api/swagger-ui





NAME
    Enable-PodeOpenApi
    
SYNOPSIS
    Enables the OpenAPI default route in Pode.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Enable-PodeOpenApi [-Path <String>] [-Title <String>] [-Version <String>] [-Description <String>] 
    [-OpenApiVersion <String>] [-RouteFilter <String>] [-EndpointName <String[]>] [-Middleware <Object[]>] 
    [-Authentication <String>] [-Role <String[]>] [-Group <String[]>] [-Scope <String[]>] [-RestrictRoutes] 
    [-Mode <String>] [-MarkupLanguage <String>] [-EnableSchemaValidation] [-Depth <Int32>] 
    [-DisableMinimalDefinitions] [-DefinitionTag <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Enable-PodeOpenApi [-Path <String>] [-OpenApiVersion <String>] [-RouteFilter <String>] [-EndpointName 
    <String[]>] [-Middleware <Object[]>] [-Authentication <String>] [-Role <String[]>] [-Group <String[]>] 
    [-Scope <String[]>] [-RestrictRoutes] [-Mode <String>] [-MarkupLanguage <String>] 
    [-EnableSchemaValidation] [-Depth <Int32>] [-DisableMinimalDefinitions] -DefaultResponses <Hashtable> 
    [-DefinitionTag <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Enable-PodeOpenApi [-Path <String>] [-OpenApiVersion <String>] [-RouteFilter <String>] [-EndpointName 
    <String[]>] [-Middleware <Object[]>] [-Authentication <String>] [-Role <String[]>] [-Group <String[]>] 
    [-Scope <String[]>] [-RestrictRoutes] [-Mode <String>] [-MarkupLanguage <String>] 
    [-EnableSchemaValidation] [-Depth <Int32>] [-DisableMinimalDefinitions] -NoDefaultResponses 
    [-DefinitionTag <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Enables the OpenAPI default route in Pode, as well as setting up details like Title and API Version.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Path <String>
        An optional custom route path to access the OpenAPI definition. (Default: /openapi)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                /openapi
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Title <String>
        The Title of the API. (Deprecated -  Use Add-PodeOAInfo)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Version <String>
        The Version of the API.   (Deprecated -  Use Add-PodeOAInfo)
        The OpenAPI Specification is versioned using Semantic Versioning 2.0.0 (semver) and follows the 
        semver specification.
        https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Description <String>
        A short description of the API. (Deprecated -  Use Add-PodeOAInfo)
        CommonMark syntax MAY be used for rich text representation.
        https://spec.commonmark.org/
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -OpenApiVersion <String>
        Specify OpenApi Version (default: 3.0.3)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                3.0.3
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -RouteFilter <String>
        An optional route filter for routes that should be included in the definition. (Default: /*)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                /*
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EndpointName <String[]>
        The EndpointName of an Endpoint(s) to bind the static Route against.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Middleware <Object[]>
        Like normal Routes, an array of Middleware that will be applied to the route.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Authentication <String>
        The name of an Authentication method which should be used as middleware on this Route.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Role <String[]>
        One or more optional Roles that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Group <String[]>
        One or more optional Groups that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Scope <String[]>
        One or more optional Scopes that will be authorised to access this Route, when using Authentication 
        with an Access method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -RestrictRoutes [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, only routes that are available on the Requests URI will be used to generate the OpenAPI 
        definition.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Mode <String>
        Define the way the OpenAPI definition file is accessed, the value can be View or Download. (Default: 
        View)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                view
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -MarkupLanguage <String>
        Define the default markup language for the OpenApi spec ('Json', 'Json-Compress', 'Yaml')
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                Json
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EnableSchemaValidation [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied enable Test-PodeOAJsonSchemaCompliance  cmdlet that provide support for opeapi parameter 
        schema validation
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Depth <Int32>
        Define the default  depth used by any JSON,YAML OpenAPI conversion (default 20)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                20
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DisableMinimalDefinitions [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied the OpenApi decument will include only the route validated by Set-PodeOARouteInfo. Any 
        other not OpenApi route will be excluded.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DefaultResponses <Hashtable>
        If supplied, it will replace the default OpenAPI response with the new provided.(Default: @{'200' = 
        @{ description = 'OK' };'default' = @{ description = 'Internal server error' }} )
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -NoDefaultResponses [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, it will disable the default OpenAPI response with the new provided.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DefinitionTag <String>
        A string representing the unique tag for the API specification.
        This tag helps distinguish between different versions or types of API specifications within the 
        application.
        You can use this tag to reference the specific API documentation, schema, or version that your 
        function interacts with.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enable-PodeOpenApi -Title 'My API' -Version '1.0.0' -RouteFilter '/api/*'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enable-PodeOpenApi -Title 'My API' -Version '1.0.0' -RouteFilter '/api/*' -RestrictRoutes
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enable-PodeOpenApi -Path '/docs/openapi'   -NoCompress -Mode 'Donwload' -DisableMinimalDefinitions
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Enable-PodeRequestLogging
    
SYNOPSIS
    Enables Request Logging using a supplied output method.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Enable-PodeRequestLogging [-Method] <Hashtable> [[-UsernameProperty] <String>] [-Raw] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Enables Request Logging using a supplied output method.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Method <Hashtable>
        The Method to use for output the log entry (From New-PodeLoggingMethod).
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -UsernameProperty <String>
        An optional property path within the $WebEvent.Auth.User object for the user's Username. (Default: 
        Username).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Raw [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the log item returned will be the raw Request item as a hashtable and not a string (for 
        Custom methods).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>New-PodeLoggingMethod -Terminal | Enable-PodeRequestLogging
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Enable-PodeServer
    
SYNOPSIS
    Enables new incoming requests by removing the middleware that blocks requests when the Pode Watchdog 
    client is active.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Enable-PodeServer [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    This function resets the cancellation token for the Disable action, allowing the Pode server to accept 
    new incoming requests.
    

PARAMETERS
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Enable-PodeSessionMiddleware
    
SYNOPSIS
    Enables Middleware for creating, retrieving and using Sessions within Pode.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Enable-PodeSessionMiddleware [-Secret <String>] [-Name <String>] [-Duration <Int32>] [-Generator 
    <ScriptBlock>] [-Storage <PSObject>] [-Scope <String>] [-Extend] [-HttpOnly] [-Secure] [-Strict] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Enable-PodeSessionMiddleware [-Secret <String>] [-Name <String>] [-Duration <Int32>] [-Generator 
    <ScriptBlock>] [-Storage <PSObject>] [-Scope <String>] [-Extend] [-Strict] [-UseHeaders] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Enables Middleware for creating, retrieving and using Sessions within Pode; with support for defining 
    Session duration, and custom Storage.
    If you're storing sessions outside of Pode, you must supply a Secret value so sessions aren't corrupted.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Secret <String>
        An optional Secret to use when signing Sessions (Default: random GUID).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Name <String>
        The name of the cookie/header used for the Session.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                pode.sid
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Duration <Int32>
        The duration a Session should last for, before being expired.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Generator <ScriptBlock>
        A custom ScriptBlock to generate a random unique SessionId. The value returned must be a String.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Storage <PSObject>
        A custom PSObject that defines methods for Delete, Get, and Set. This allow you to store Sessions in 
        custom Storage such as Redis. A Secret is required.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Scope <String>
        The Scope that the Session applies to, possible values are Browser and Tab (Default: Browser).
        The Browser scope is the default logic, where authentication and general data for the sessions are 
        shared across all tabs.
        The Tab scope keep the authentication data shared across all tabs, but general data is separated 
        across different tabs.
        For the Tab scope, the "Tab ID" required will be sourced from the "X-PODE-SESSION-TAB-ID" header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                Browser
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Extend [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the Sessions will have their durations extended on each successful Request.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -HttpOnly [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the Session cookie will only be accessible to browsers.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Secure [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the Session cookie will only be accessible over HTTPS Requests.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Strict [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the Secret will be extended using the client request's UserAgent and RemoteIPAddress.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -UseHeaders [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, Sessions will be sent back in a header on the Response with the Name supplied.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enable-PodeSessionMiddleware -Duration 120
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enable-PodeSessionMiddleware -Duration 120 -Extend -Generator { return 
    [System.IO.Path]::GetRandomFileName() }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enable-PodeSessionMiddleware -Secret 'schwifty' -Duration 120 -UseHeaders -Strict
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Enable-PodeSignalSigning
    
SYNOPSIS

PARAMETERS

        A Secret to sign ClientIds, Get-PodeServerDefaultSecret can be used.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Strict [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the Secret will be extended using the client request's UserAgent and RemoteIPAddress.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enable-PodeSignalSigning
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enable-PodeSignalSigning -Strict
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enable-PodeSignalSigning -Secret 'Sup3rS3cr37!' -Strict
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enable-PodeSignalSigning -Secret 'Sup3rS3cr37!'

NAME
    Enter-PodeLockable
    
SYNOPSIS
    Place a lock on an object or Lockable.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Enter-PodeLockable [[-Object] <Object>] [-Timeout <Int32>] [-CheckGlobal] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Enter-PodeLockable -Name <String> [-Timeout <Int32>] [-CheckGlobal] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Place a lock on an object or Lockable. This should eventually be followed by a call to Exit-PodeLockable.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Object <Object>
        The Object, or Lockable, to lock. If no Object is supplied then the global lockable is used by 
        default.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Name <String>
        The Name of a Lockable object in Pode to lock, if no Name is supplied then the global lockable is 
        used by default.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Timeout <Int32>
        If supplied, a number of milliseconds to timeout after if a lock cannot be acquired. (Default: 
        Infinite)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                -1
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -CheckGlobal [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, will check the global Lockable object and wait until it's freed-up before locking the 
        passed object.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enter-PodeLockable -Object $SomeArray
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enter-PodeLockable -Name 'LockName' -Timeout 5000
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Enter-PodeMutex
    
SYNOPSIS
    Acquires a hold on a Mutex.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Enter-PodeMutex [-Name] <String> [[-Timeout] <Int32>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Acquires a hold on a Mutex. This should eventually by followed by a call to Exit-PodeMutex.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Mutex.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Timeout <Int32>
        If supplied, a number of milliseconds to timeout after if a hold cannot be acquired on the Mutex. 
        (Default: Infinite)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                -1
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enter-PodeMutex -Name 'SelfMutex' -Timeout 5000
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Enter-PodeSemaphore
    
SYNOPSIS
    Acquires a hold on a Semaphore.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Enter-PodeSemaphore [-Name] <String> [[-Timeout] <Int32>] [<CommonParameters>]

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Semaphore.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Timeout <Int32>
        If supplied, a number of milliseconds to timeout after if a hold cannot be acquired on the Semaphore. 
        (Default: Infinite)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                -1
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Enter-PodeSemaphore -Name 'SelfSemaphore' -Timeout 5000
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Exit-PodeLockable
    
SYNOPSIS
    Remove a lock from an object or Lockable.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Exit-PodeLockable [[-Object] <Object>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Exit-PodeLockable -Name <String> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Remove a lock from an object or Lockable, that was originally locked via Enter-PodeLockable.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Object <Object>
        The Object, or Lockable, to unlock. If no Object is supplied then the global lockable is used by 
        default.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Name <String>
        The Name of a Lockable object in Pode to unlock, if no Name is supplied then the global lockable is 
        used by default.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Exit-PodeLockable -Object $SomeArray
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Exit-PodeLockable -Name 'LockName'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Exit-PodeMutex

    Release the hold on a Mutex.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Exit-PodeMutex [-Name] <String> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Release the hold on a Mutex, that was originally acquired by Enter-PodeMutex.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Mutex.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Exit-PodeMutex -Name 'SelfMutex'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Exit-PodeSemaphore
    
SYNOPSIS
    Release the hold on a Semaphore.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Exit-PodeSemaphore [-Name] <String> [[-ReleaseCount] <Int32>] [<CommonParameters>]

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Semaphore.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ReleaseCount <Int32>
        The number of releases to release in one go. (Default: 1)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                1
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Exit-PodeSemaphore -Name 'SelfSemaphore'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

    Get one or more Access methods.

        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS

    System.Object[]
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------

    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------

NAME
    Get-PodeAuthEvent
    
SYNOPSIS
    Gets registered Authentication events.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeAuthEvent [[-Name] <String[]>] [-Type] <String[]> [[-EventName] <String[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Gets registered Authentication events.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String[]>
        An optional array of Authentication method Names to get events for.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Type <String[]>
        The Type of event to get, either 'Login' or 'Logout'.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EventName <String[]>
        An optional array of event Names to get.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Object[]
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$events = Get-PodeAuthEvent -Name 'Basic' -Type 'Login' -EventName 'OnLogin'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$events = Get-PodeAuthEvent -Type 'Logout'

NAME
    Get-PodeAuthUser
    
SYNOPSIS
    Get the authenticated user from the WebEvent or Session.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeAuthUser [-IgnoreSession] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Get the authenticated user from the WebEvent or Session. This is similar to calling $Webevent.Auth.User.
    

PARAMETERS
    -IgnoreSession [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, only the Auth object in the WebEvent will be used and the Session will be skipped.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$user = Get-PodeAuthUser
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Get-PodeCache
    
SYNOPSIS
    Return the value of a key from the cache. You can use "$value = $cache:key" as well.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeCache [-Key] <String> [[-Storage] <String>] [-Metadata] [-NoEnumerate] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Return the value of a key from the cache, or returns the value plus metadata such as expiry time if 
    required. You can use "$value = $cache:key" as well.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Key <String>
        The Key to be retrieved.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Storage <String>
        An optional cache Storage name. (Default: in-memory)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Metadata [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, and if supported by the cache storage, an metadata such as expiry times will also be 
        returned.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -NoEnumerate [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, and if the value returned from the cache is an IEnumerable, it will not be unwrapped per 
        normal PowerShell behaviour.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Object[]
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$value = Get-PodeCache -Key 'ExampleKey'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$value = Get-PodeCache -Key 'ExampleKey' -Storage 'ExampleStorage'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$value = Get-PodeCache -Key 'ExampleKey' -Metadata
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$value = Get-PodeCache -Key 'ExampleKey' -NoEnumerate
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 5 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$value = $cache:ExampleKey
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

NAME
    Get-PodeCookie
    
SYNOPSIS
    Retrieves a cookie from the Request.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeCookie [-Name] <String> [[-Secret] <String>] [-Strict] [-Raw] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Retrieves a cookie from the Request, with the option to supply a secret to unsign the cookie's value.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The name of the cookie to retrieve.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Secret <String>
        The secret used to unsign the cookie's value.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Strict [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the Secret will be extended using the client request's UserAgent and RemoteIPAddress.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Raw [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the cookie returned will be the raw .NET Cookie object for manipulation.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Collections.Hashtable
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeCookie -Name 'Views'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeCookie -Name 'Views' -Secret 'hunter2'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Get-PodeCookieSecret

    Get-PodeCookieSecret [-Global] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION

        Required?                    true

        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Global [<SwitchParameter>]
        If flagged, will return the current global secret value.
        
        Required?                    false

        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.String
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------

    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeCookieSecret -Global

NAME
    Get-PodeCsrfMiddleware
    
SYNOPSIS
    Returns adhoc CSRF CSRF verification Middleware, for use on Routes.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeCsrfMiddleware [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Returns adhoc CSRF CSRF verification Middleware, for use on Routes.
    

PARAMETERS
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Collections.Hashtable
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$csrf = Get-PodeCsrfMiddleware
    
    Add-PodeRoute -Method Get -Path '/cpu' -Middleware $csrf -ScriptBlock { /* logic */ }
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Get-PodeCurrentRunspaceName
    
SYNOPSIS
    Retrieves the name of the current PowerShell runspace.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeCurrentRunspaceName [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    The Get-PodeCurrentRunspaceName function retrieves the name of the current PowerShell runspace.
    This can be useful for debugging or logging purposes to identify the runspace in use.
    

PARAMETERS
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
NOTES
    
    
        This is an internal function and may change in future releases of Pode.
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeCurrentRunspaceName
    
    Returns the name of the current runspace.
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Get-PodeDefaultFolder
    
SYNOPSIS
    Retrieves the path of a specified default folder type from the Pode server context.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeDefaultFolder [[-Type] <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    This function returns the path for one of the Pode server's default folder types: Views, Public, or 
    Errors. It accesses the server's configuration stored in the `$PodeContext` variable and retrieves the 
    path for the specified folder type from the `DefaultFolders` dictionary. This function is useful for 
    scripts or modules that need to dynamically access server resources based on the server's current 
    configuration.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Type <String>
        The type of the default folder for which to retrieve the path. The valid options are 'Views', 
        'Public', or 'Errors'. This parameter determines which folder's path will be returned by the function.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    String. The file system path of the specified default folder.
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$path = Get-PodeDefaultFolder -Type 'Views'
    
    This example retrieves the current path configured for the server's 'Views' folder and stores it in the 
    `$path` variable.
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$path = Get-PodeDefaultFolder -Type 'Public'
    
    This example retrieves the current path configured for the server's 'Public' folder.
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Get-PodeEndpoint
    
SYNOPSIS
    Get an Endpoint(s).
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeEndpoint [[-Address] <String>] [[-Port] <Int32>] [[-Hostname] <String>] [[-Protocol] <String>] 
    [[-Name] <String[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Get an Endpoint(s).
    

PARAMETERS
    -Address <String>
        An Address to filter the endpoints.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Port <Int32>
        A Port to filter the endpoints.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Hostname <String>
        A Hostname to filter the endpoints.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Protocol <String>
        A Protocol to filter the endpoints.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    4
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Name <String[]>
        Any endpoints Names to filter endpoints.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    5
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeEndpoint -Address 127.0.0.1
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeEndpoint -Protocol Http
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeEndpoint -Name Admin, User
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Get-PodeEvent
    
SYNOPSIS
    Retrieves one or more events of a specified Type.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeEvent [-Type] {Start | Starting | Terminate | Restarting | Restart | Browser | Crash | Stop | 
    Running | Suspending | Suspend | Resuming | Resume | Enable | Disable} [[-Name] <String[]>] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Retrieves one or more events of a specified Type, and optionally by Name.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Type
        One of more event Types to retrieve.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Name <String[]>
        AN optional list of event Names to retrieve.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Object[]
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeEvent -Type Start -Name 'Event1'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeEvent -Type Start, Stop
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeEvent -Type Start, Stop -Name 'Event1', 'Event2'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Get-PodeFavicon
    
SYNOPSIS
    Retrieves the configured favicon(s) for one or more Pode endpoints.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeFavicon [[-EndpointName] <String>] [-DefaultEndpoint] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    This function returns a hashtable containing the endpoint names and their corresponding favicon data
    (byte array and content type). If an endpoint name is specified, only its favicon is returned if set.
    If none is specified, the function returns all favicons set across endpoints. You can also limit the
    query to only endpoints marked as default.
    

PARAMETERS
    -EndpointName <String>
        The name of a specific endpoint to retrieve the favicon from. If not provided, favicons from all 
        endpoints are returned.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DefaultEndpoint [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, only endpoints created with `-Default` are included in the result.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Collections.Hashtable
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeFavicon
    
    # Returns a hashtable of all endpoints with configured favicons.
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeFavicon -EndpointName 'api'
    
    # Returns the favicon for the 'api' endpoint.
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeFavicon -DefaultEndpoint
    
    # Returns favicons for endpoints marked as default only.
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

    Get-PodeFileWatcher

INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    A hashtable array containing the access rule(s).
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$rules = Get-PodeLimitAccessRule -Name 'rule1'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$rules = Get-PodeLimitAccessRule -Name 'rule1', 'rule2'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$rules = Get-PodeLimitAccessRule
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS
