NAME
    Get-PodeMcpGroup
    
SYNOPSIS
    Retrieves an MCP tool group or groups.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeMcpGroup [[-Name] <String[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Retrieves an MCP tool group or groups from the server.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String[]>
        The Name of the MCP tool group to retrieve. If not specified, all groups will be returned.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Collections.Hashtable
    
    System.Collections.Hashtable[]
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\># retrieve a specific MCP tool group by name
    
    Get-PodeMcpGroup -Name 'default'
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Get-PodeMcpTool
    
SYNOPSIS
    Retrieves an MCP tool(s).
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeMcpTool [[-Name] <String[]>] [[-Group] <String[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Retrieves an MCP tool or tools from the server based on the provided name and group.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String[]>
        The Name of the MCP tool to retrieve. If not specified, all tools in the specified group will be 
        returned.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Group <String[]>
        The Group(s) from which to retrieve the MCP tool(s).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Collections.Hashtable
    
    System.Collections.Hashtable[]
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\># retrieve a specific MCP tool by name from a default group
    
    Get-PodeMcpTool -Name 'GreetPerson' -Group 'default'
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\># retrieve all MCP tools from a specific group
    
    Get-PodeMcpTool -Group 'custom'
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\># attempt to retrieve a non-existent tool, which will return null
    
    Get-PodeMcpTool -Name 'NonExistentTool'
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\># attempt to retrieve tools from a non-existent group, which will return null
    
    Get-PodeMcpTool -Group 'NonExistentGroup'
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

NAME
    Get-PodeOADefinition
    
SYNOPSIS
    Gets the OpenAPI definition.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeOADefinition [[-Format] <String>] [[-Title] <String>] [[-Version] <String>] [[-Description] 
    <String>] [[-RouteFilter] <String>] [-RestrictRoutes] [[-DefinitionTag] <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Gets the OpenAPI definition for custom use in routes, or other functions.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Format <String>
        Return the definition  in a specific format 'Json', 'Json-Compress', 'Yaml', 'HashTable'
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                HashTable
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Title <String>
        The Title of the API. (Default: the title supplied in Enable-PodeOpenApi)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Version <String>
        The Version of the API. (Default: the version supplied in Enable-PodeOpenApi)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Description <String>
        A Description of the API. (Default: the description supplied into Enable-PodeOpenApi)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    4
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -RouteFilter <String>
        An optional route filter for routes that should be included in the definition. (Default: /*)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    5
        Default value                /*
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -RestrictRoutes [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, only routes that are available on the Requests URI will be used to generate the OpenAPI 
        definition.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DefinitionTag <String>
        A string representing the unique tag for the API specification.
        This tag helps distinguish between different versions or types of API specifications within the 
        application.
        You can use this tag to reference the specific API documentation, schema, or version that your 
        function interacts with.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    6
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$defInJson = Get-PodeOADefinition -Json
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Get-PodeRoute
    
SYNOPSIS
    Get a Route(s).
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeRoute [[-Method] <String>] [[-Path] <String>] [[-EndpointName] <String[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Get a Route(s).
    

PARAMETERS
    -Method <String>
        A Method to filter the routes.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Path <String>
        A Path to filter the routes.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EndpointName <String[]>
        The name of an endpoint to filter routes.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Object[]
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeRoute -Method Get -Path '/about'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------

NAME
    Get-PodeSchedule
    
SYNOPSIS
    Returns any defined schedules.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeSchedule [[-Name] <String[]>] [[-StartTime] <Object>] [[-EndTime] <Object>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Returns any defined schedules, with support for filtering.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String[]>
        Any schedule Names to filter the schedules.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -StartTime <Object>
        An optional StartTime to only return Schedules that will trigger after this date.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EndTime <Object>
        An optional EndTime to only return Schedules that will trigger before this date.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeSchedule
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeSchedule -Name Name1, Name2
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeSchedule -Name Name1, Name2 -StartTime [datetime]::new(2020, 3, 1) -EndTime 
    [datetime]::new(2020, 3, 31)
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Get-PodeScheduleNextTrigger
    
SYNOPSIS
    Get the next trigger time for a Schedule.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeScheduleNextTrigger [-Name] <String> [[-DateTime] <Object>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Get the next trigger time for a Schedule, either from the Schedule's StartTime or from a defined DateTime.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Schedule.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DateTime <Object>
        An optional specific DateTime to get the next trigger time after. This DateTime must be between the 
        Schedule's StartTime and EndTime.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeScheduleNextTrigger -Name Schedule1
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeScheduleNextTrigger -Name Schedule1 -DateTime [datetime]::new(2020, 3, 10)
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

NAME

    Retrieve the value of a mounted Secret.

    Retrieve the value of a mounted Secret from a Secret Vault. You can also use "$value = $secret:<NAME>" 
    syntax in certain places.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The friendly Name of a Secret.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$value = Get-PodeSecret -Name 'SecretName'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$value = $secret:SecretName

    PS C:\>Get-PodeServerActiveSignalMetric

        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Int64
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$count = Get-PodeServerActiveSseMetric
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

NAME
    Get-PodeServerRequestMetric
    
SYNOPSIS
    Returns the total number of requests/per status code the Server has received.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeServerRequestMetric [-StatusCode <Int32>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Get-PodeServerRequestMetric [-Total] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Returns the total number of requests/per status code the Server has received.
    

PARAMETERS
    -StatusCode <Int32>
        If supplied, will return the total number of requests for a specific StatusCode.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Total [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, will return the Total number of Requests.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Int64
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$totalReqs = Get-PodeServerRequestMetric -Total
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$statusReqs = Get-PodeServerRequestMetric
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$404Reqs = Get-PodeServerRequestMetric -StatusCode 404
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

NAME
    Get-PodeServerState
    
SYNOPSIS
    Retrieves the current state of the Pode server.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeServerState [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    The Get-PodeServerState function evaluates the internal state of the Pode server based on the 
    cancellation tokens available
    in the $PodeContext. The function determines if the server is running, terminating, restarting, 
    suspending, resuming, or
    in any other predefined state.
    

PARAMETERS
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    [string] - The state of the Pode server as one of the following values:
               'Terminated', 'Terminating', 'Resuming', 'Suspending', 'Suspended', 'Restarting', 'Starting', 
    'Running'.
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeServerState
    
    Retrieves the current state of the Pode server and returns it as a string.
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Get-PodeServerUptime
    
SYNOPSIS
    Retrieves the server uptime in milliseconds or a human-readable format.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeServerUptime [-Total] [[-Format] <String>] [-ExcludeMilliseconds] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    The `Get-PodeServerUptime` function calculates the server's uptime since its last start or total uptime 
    since initial load, depending on the `-Total` switch.
    By default, the uptime is returned in milliseconds. When the `-Format` parameter is used, the uptime can 
    be returned in various human-readable styles:
    - `Milliseconds` (default): Raw uptime in milliseconds.
    - `Concise`: A short format like "1d 2h 3m".
    - `Compact`: A condensed format like "01:10:17:36".
    - `Verbose`: A detailed format like "1 day, 2 hours, 3 minutes, 5 seconds, 200 milliseconds".
    The `-ExcludeMilliseconds` switch allows removal of milliseconds from human-readable output.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Total [<SwitchParameter>]
        Retrieves the total server uptime since the initial load, regardless of any restarts.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Format <String>
        Specifies the desired output format for the uptime.
        Allowed values:
        - `Milliseconds` (default): Uptime in raw milliseconds.
        - `Concise`: Human-readable in a short form (e.g., "1d 2h 3m").
        - `Compact`: Condensed form (e.g., "01:10:17:36").
        - `Verbose`: Detailed format (e.g., "1 day, 2 hours, 3 minutes, 5 seconds").
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                Milliseconds
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ExcludeMilliseconds [<SwitchParameter>]
        Omits milliseconds from the human-readable output when `-Format` is not `Milliseconds`.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Int64
    
    System.String
    
    
NOTES
    
    
        This function is part of Pode's utility metrics to monitor server uptime.
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$currentUptime = Get-PodeServerUptime
    
    # Output: 123456789 (milliseconds)
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$totalUptime = Get-PodeServerUptime -Total
    
    # Output: 987654321 (milliseconds)
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$readableUptime = Get-PodeServerUptime -Format Concise
    
    # Output: "1d 10h 17m"
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$verboseUptime = Get-PodeServerUptime -Format Verbose
    
    # Output: "1 day, 10 hours, 17 minutes, 36 seconds, 789 milliseconds"
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 5 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$compactUptime = Get-PodeServerUptime -Format Compact
    
    # Output: "01:10:17:36"
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 6 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$compactUptimeNoMs = Get-PodeServerUptime -Format Compact -ExcludeMilliseconds
    
    # Output: "01:10:17:36"
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

NAME
    Get-PodeSessionId
    
SYNOPSIS
    Returns the currently authenticated SessionId.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeSessionId [-Signed] [-Force] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Returns the currently authenticated SessionId. If there's no session, or it's not authenticated, then 
    null is returned instead.
    You can also have the SessionId returned as signed as well.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Signed [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the returned SessionId will also be signed.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Force [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the sessionId will be returned regardless of authentication.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$sessionId = Get-PodeSessionId
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String[]>
        An optional Name of the Signal connection(s) to retrieve events for.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Type
        The Type of event to retrieve, either Connect or Disconnect.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EventName <String[]>
        An optional name of the event being retrieved.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Object[]
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$events = Get-PodeSignalEvent -Name 'Metrics' -Type Connect -EventName 'OnConnect'

RELATED LINKS

        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Type
        The Type(s) of event to retrieve, either Connect or Disconnect.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EventName <String[]>
        An optional name(s) of the event to retrieve.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Object[]
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$events = Get-PodeSseEvent -Name 'Actions' -Type Connect -EventName 'OnConnect'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$events = Get-PodeSseEvent -Type Disconnect
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

    -WithScope [<SwitchParameter>]

        Accept pipeline input?       false

    <CommonParameters>

        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,

NAME
    Get-PodeStateNames
    
SYNOPSIS
    Returns the current names of state variables.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeStateNames [[-Pattern] <String>] [[-Scope] <String[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Returns the current names of state variables that have been set. You can filter the result using Scope or 
    a Pattern.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Pattern <String>
        An optional regex Pattern to filter the state names.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Scope <String[]>
        An optional Scope to filter the state names.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    $names = Get-PodeStateNames -Scope '<scope>'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$names = Get-PodeStateNames -Pattern '^\w+[0-9]{0,2}$'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

NAME
    Get-PodeTaskProcess
    
SYNOPSIS
    Get all Task Processes.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeTaskProcess [[-Name] <String[]>] [[-Id] <String[]>] [[-State] <String[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Get all Task Processes, with support for filtering. These are the processes created when using 
    Invoke-PodeTask.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String[]>
        An optional Name of the Task to filter by, can be one or more.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Id <String[]>
        An optional ID of the Task process to filter by, can be one or more.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -State <String[]>
        An optional State of the Task process to filter by, can be one or more.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                All
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeTaskProcess
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeTaskProcess -Name 'TaskName'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeTaskProcess -Id 'TaskId'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Get-PodeTaskProcess -State 'Running'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

NAME
    Get-PodeVersion
    
SYNOPSIS
    Gets the version of the Pode module.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Get-PodeVersion [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    The Get-PodeVersion function checks the version of the Pode module specified in the module manifest. If 
    the module version is not a placeholder value ('$version$'), it returns the actual version prefixed with 
    'v.'. If the module version is the placeholder value, indicating the development branch, it returns 
    '[develop branch]'.
    

PARAMETERS
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.String
    Returns a string indicating the version of the Pode module or '[dev]' if on a development version.
    
    
NOTES
    
    
        This function assumes that $moduleManifest is a hashtable representing the loaded module manifest, 
        with a key of ModuleVersion.
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS>$moduleManifest = @{ ModuleVersion = '1.2.3' }
    
    PS> Get-PodeVersion
    
    Returns 'v1.2.3'.
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS>$moduleManifest = @{ ModuleVersion = '$version$' }
    
    PS> Get-PodeVersion
    
    Returns '[dev]'.
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

    PS C:\>Hide-PodeSecurityServer

NAME
    Import-PodeModule
    
SYNOPSIS
    Imports a Module into the current, and all runspaces that Pode uses.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Import-PodeModule -Name <String> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Import-PodeModule -Path <String> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Imports a Module into the current, and all runspaces that Pode uses. Modules can also be imported from 
    the ps_modules directory.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The name of a globally installed Module, or one within the ps_modules directory, to import.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Path <String>
        The path, literal or relative, to a Module to import.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Import-PodeModule -Name IISManager
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Import-PodeModule -Path './modules/utilities.psm1'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

NAME
    Initialize-PodeCsrf
    
SYNOPSIS
    Initialises CSRF within Pode for adhoc usage.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Initialize-PodeCsrf [[-IgnoreMethods] <String[]>] [[-Secret] <String>] [-UseCookies] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Initialises CSRF within Pode for adhoc usage, with configurable HTTP methods to ignore verification.
    

PARAMETERS
    -IgnoreMethods <String[]>
        An array of HTTP methods to ignore CSRF verification.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                @('Get', 'Head', 'Options', 'Trace')
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Secret <String>
        A secret to use when signing cookies - for when using CSRF with cookies.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -UseCookies [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, CSRF will used cookies rather than sessions.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Initialize-PodeCsrf -IgnoreMethods @('Get', 'Trace')
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Initialize-PodeCsrf -Secret 'some-secret' -UseCookies
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS
