SYNTAX
    New-PodeOAStringProperty [[-ParamsList] <Hashtable[]>] [-Name <String>] [-Format <String>] [-Default 
    <String>] [-Pattern <String>] [-Description <String>] [-ExternalDoc <String>] [-Example <Object>] [-Enum 
    <String[]>] [-Required] [-Deprecated] [-Object] [-Nullable] [-ReadOnly] [-WriteOnly] [-MinLength <Int32>] 
    [-MaxLength <Int32>] [-NoAdditionalProperties] [-AdditionalProperties <Hashtable>] [-XmlName <String>] 
    [-XmlNamespace <String>] [-XmlPrefix <String>] [-XmlAttribute] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    New-PodeOAStringProperty [[-ParamsList] <Hashtable[]>] [-Name <String>] [-Format <String>] [-CustomFormat 
    <String>] [-Default <String>] [-Pattern <String>] [-Description <String>] [-ExternalDoc <String>] 
    [-Example <Object>] [-Enum <String[]>] [-Required] [-Deprecated] [-Object] [-Nullable] [-ReadOnly] 
    [-WriteOnly] [-MinLength <Int32>] [-MaxLength <Int32>] [-NoAdditionalProperties] [-AdditionalProperties 
    <Hashtable>] [-XmlName <String>] [-XmlNamespace <String>] [-XmlPrefix <String>] [-XmlAttribute] 
    [-XmlItemName <String>] [-XmlWrapped] -Array [-UniqueItems] [-MinItems <Int32>] [-MaxItems <Int32>] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    New-PodeOAStringProperty [[-ParamsList] <Hashtable[]>] [-Name <String>] [-CustomFormat <String>] 
    [-Default <String>] [-Pattern <String>] [-Description <String>] [-ExternalDoc <String>] [-Example 
    <Object>] [-Enum <String[]>] [-Required] [-Deprecated] [-Object] [-Nullable] [-ReadOnly] [-WriteOnly] 
    [-MinLength <Int32>] [-MaxLength <Int32>] [-NoAdditionalProperties] [-AdditionalProperties <Hashtable>] 
    [-XmlName <String>] [-XmlNamespace <String>] [-XmlPrefix <String>] [-XmlAttribute] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Creates a new OpenAPI string property, for Schemas or Parameters.
    

PARAMETERS
    -ParamsList <Hashtable[]>
        Used to pipeline multiple properties
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the property.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Format <String>
        The inbuilt OpenAPI Format of the string. (Default: Any)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -CustomFormat <String>
        The name of a custom OpenAPI Format of the string. (Default: None)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Default <String>
        The default value of the property. (Default: $null)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Pattern <String>
        A Regex pattern that the string must match.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Description <String>
        A Description of the property.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ExternalDoc <String>
        If supplied, add an additional external documentation for this operation.
        The parameter is created by Add-PodeOAExternalDoc
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Example <Object>
        An example of a parameter value
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Enum <String[]>
        An optional array of values that this property can only be set to.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Required [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the string will be treated as Required where supported.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Deprecated [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the string will be treated as Deprecated where supported.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Object [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the string will be automatically wrapped in an object.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Nullable [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the string will be treated as Nullable.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ReadOnly [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the string will be included in a response but not in a request
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -WriteOnly [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the string will be included in a request but not in a response
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -MinLength <Int32>
        If supplied, the string will be restricted to minimal length of characters.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -MaxLength <Int32>
        If supplied, the string will be restricted to maximal length of characters.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -NoAdditionalProperties [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, will configure the OpenAPI property additionalProperties to false.
        This means that the defined object will not allow any properties beyond those explicitly declared in 
        its schema.
        If any additional properties are provided, they will be considered invalid.
        Use this switch to enforce a strict schema definition, ensuring that objects contain only the 
        specified set of properties and no others.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -AdditionalProperties <Hashtable>
        Define a set of additional properties for the OpenAPI schema. This parameter accepts a HashTable 
        where each key-value pair represents a property name and its corresponding schema.
        The schema for each property can include type, format, description, and other OpenAPI specification 
        attributes.
        When specified, these additional properties are included in the OpenAPI definition, allowing for more 
        flexible and dynamic object structures.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -XmlName <String>
        By default, XML elements get the same names that fields in the API declaration have. This property 
        change the XML name of the property
        reflecting the 'xml.name' attribute in the OpenAPI specification.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -XmlNamespace <String>
        Defines a specific XML namespace for the property, corresponding to the 'xml.namespace' attribute in 
        OpenAPI.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -XmlPrefix <String>
        Sets a prefix for the XML element name, aligning with the 'xml.prefix' attribute in OpenAPI.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -XmlAttribute [<SwitchParameter>]
        Indicates whether the property should be serialized as an XML attribute, equivalent to the 
        'xml.attribute' attribute in OpenAPI.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -XmlItemName <String>
        Specifically for properties treated as arrays, it defines the XML name for each item in the array. 
        This parameter aligns with the 'xml.name' attribute under 'items' in OpenAPI.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -XmlWrapped [<SwitchParameter>]
        Indicates whether array items should be wrapped in an XML element, similar to the 'xml.wrapped' 
        attribute in OpenAPI.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Array [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the object will be treated as an array of objects.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -UniqueItems [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, specify that all items in the array must be unique
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -MinItems <Int32>
        If supplied, specify minimum length of an array
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -MaxItems <Int32>
        If supplied, specify maximum length of an array
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>New-PodeOAStringProperty -Name 'userType' -Default 'admin'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>New-PodeOAStringProperty -Name 'password' -Format Password

NAME
    New-PodeSemaphore
    
SYNOPSIS
    Create a new Semaphore.
    
    
SYNTAX
    New-PodeSemaphore [-Name] <String> [[-Count] <Int32>] [[-Scope] <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Create a new Semaphore.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Semaphore.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Count <Int32>
        The number of threads to allow a hold on the Semaphore. (Default: 1)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                1
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Scope <String>
        The Scope of the Semaphore, can be either Self, Local, or Global. (Default: Self)
        Self: The current process, or child processes.
        Local: All processes for the current login session on Windows, or the the same as Self on Unix.
        Global: All processes on the system, across every session.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                Self
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>New-PodeSemaphore -Name 'SelfSemaphore'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>New-PodeSemaphore -Name 'LocalSemaphore' -Scope Local
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>New-PodeSemaphore -Name 'GlobalSemaphore' -Count 3 -Scope Global
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    New-PodeSignalClientId
    
SYNOPSIS
    Generate a new Signal connection ClientId.
    
    
SYNTAX
    New-PodeSignalClientId [[-ClientId] <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Generate a new Signal connection ClientId, which will be signed if signing enabled.
    

PARAMETERS
    -ClientId <String>
        An optional Signal connection ClientId to use, if a custom ClientId is needed and required to be 
        signed.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$clientId = New-PodeSignalClientId
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$clientId = New-PodeSignalClientId -ClientId 'my-client-id'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$clientId = New-PodeSignalClientId -ClientId 
    's:my-already-signed-client-id.uvG49LcojTMuJ0l4yzBzr6jCqEV8gGC/0YgsYU1QEuQ='
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

    New-PodeSseClientId

PARAMETERS
    -ClientId <String>
        An optional SSE connection ClientId to use, if a custom ClientId is needed and required to be signed.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$clientId = New-PodeSseClientId
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$clientId = New-PodeSseClientId -ClientId 'my-client-id'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$clientId = New-PodeSseClientId -ClientId 
    's:my-already-signed-client-id.uvG49LcojTMuJ0l4yzBzr6jCqEV8gGC/0YgsYU1QEuQ='

NAME
    Out-PodeHost
    
SYNOPSIS
    Outputs an object to the main Host.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Out-PodeHost [-InputObject] <Object> [-Force] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Due to Pode's use of runspaces, this will output a given object back to the main Host.
    It's advised to use this function over Out-Default, so that any output respects the -Quiet flag of the 
    server.
    

PARAMETERS
    -InputObject <Object>
        The object to output.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Force [<SwitchParameter>]
        Overrides the -Quiet flag of the server.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>'Hello, world!' | Out-PodeHost
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>@{ Name = 'Rick' } | Out-PodeHost
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Out-PodeVariable
    
SYNOPSIS
    Defines variables to be created when the Pode server stops.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Out-PodeVariable -Name <String> [[-Value] <Object>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Allows you to define a variable, with a value, that should be created on the in the main scope after the 
    Pode server is stopped.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the variable to be set
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Value <Object>
        The Value of the variable to be set
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Out-PodeVariable -Name ExampleVar -Value @{ Name = 'Bob' }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Pode
    
SYNOPSIS
    The CLI for Pode, to initialise, build and start your Server.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Pode [-Action] <String> [-Dev] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    The CLI for Pode, to initialise, build and start your Server.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Action <String>
        The action to invoke on your Server.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Dev [<SwitchParameter>]
        Supply when running "pode install", this will install any dev packages defined in your package.json.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>pode install -dev
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>pode build
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>pode start
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Protect-PodeLogItem
    
SYNOPSIS
    Masks values within a log item to protect sensitive information.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Protect-PodeLogItem [[-Item] <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Masks values within a log item, or any string, to protect sensitive information.
    Patterns, and the Mask, can be configured via the server.psd1 configuration file.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Item <String>
        The string Item to mask values.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.String
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$value = Protect-PodeLogItem -Item 'Username=Morty, Password=Hunter2'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Protect-PodeValue
    
SYNOPSIS
    Resolves and protects a value by ensuring it defaults to a specified fallback and optionally parses it as 
    an enum.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Protect-PodeValue [[-Value] <Object>] [[-Default] <Object>] [[-EnumType] <Type>] [-CaseSensitive] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    The `Protect-PodeValue` function ensures that a given value is resolved. If the value is empty, a default 
    value is used instead.
    Additionally, the function can parse the resolved value as an enum type with optional case sensitivity.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Value <Object>
        The input value to be resolved.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Default <Object>
        The default value to fall back to if the input value is empty.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EnumType <Type>
        The type of enum to parse the resolved value into. If specified, the resolved value must be a valid 
        enum member.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -CaseSensitive [<SwitchParameter>]
        Specifies whether the enum parsing should be case-sensitive. By default, parsing is case-insensitive.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    [object]
    Returns the resolved value, either as the original value, the default value, or a parsed enum.
    
    
NOTES
    
    
        This function resolves values using `Resolve-PodeValue` and validates enums using `[enum]::IsDefined`.
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\># Example 1: Resolve a value with a default fallback
    
    $resolved = Protect-PodeValue -Value $null -Default "Fallback"
    Write-Output $resolved  # Output: Fallback
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\># Example 2: Resolve and parse a value as a case-insensitive enum
    
    $resolvedEnum = Protect-PodeValue -Value "red" -Default "Blue" -EnumType ([type][System.ConsoleColor])
    Write-Output $resolvedEnum  # Output: Red
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\># Example 3: Resolve and parse a value as a case-sensitive enum
    
    $resolvedEnum = Protect-PodeValue -Value "red" -Default "Blue" -EnumType ([type][System.ConsoleColor]) 
    -CaseSensitive
    # Throws an error if "red" does not match an enum member exactly (case-sensitive).
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Read-PodeSecret
    
SYNOPSIS
    Read a Secret from a Secret Vault.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Read-PodeSecret [-Key] <String> [-Vault] <String> [[-Property] <String[]>] [[-ExpandProperty] <String>] 
    [[-ArgumentList] <Object[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Read a Secret from a Secret Vault.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Key <String>
        The Key/Path of the Secret within the Secret Vault.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Vault <String>
        The friendly name of the Secret Vault this Secret can be found in.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Property <String[]>
        An optional array of Properties to be returned if the Secret contains multiple properties.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ExpandProperty <String>
        An optional Property to be expanded from the Secret and return if it contains multiple properties.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    4
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ArgumentList <Object[]>
        An optional array of Arguments to be supplied to a custom Secret Vault's scriptblocks.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    5
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$value = Read-PodeSecret -Key 'path/to/secret' -Vault 'VaultName'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$value = Read-PodeSecret -Key 'key_of_secret' -Vault 'VaultName' -Property prop1, prop2
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Read-PodeTcpClient
    
SYNOPSIS
    Reads data from a TCP socket stream.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Read-PodeTcpClient [-Timeout <Int32>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Read-PodeTcpClient [-Timeout <Int32>] [-CheckBytes <Byte[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Read-PodeTcpClient [-Timeout <Int32>] [-CRLFMessageEnd] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Reads data from a TCP socket stream.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Timeout <Int32>
        An optional Timeout in milliseconds.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -CheckBytes <Byte[]>
        An optional array of bytes to check at the end of a received data stream, to determine if the data is 
        complete.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -CRLFMessageEnd [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the CheckBytes will be set to 13 and 10 to make sure a message ends with CR and LF.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.String
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$data = Read-PodeTcpClient
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$data = Read-PodeTcpClient -CRLFMessageEnd
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Register-PodeAuthEvent
    
SYNOPSIS
    Registers an Authentication event ScriptBlock.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Register-PodeAuthEvent [-Name] <String[]> [-Type] <String> [-EventName] <String> [-ScriptBlock] 
    <ScriptBlock> [[-ArgumentList] <Object[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Registers an Authentication event ScriptBlock, allowing custom code to be run on Login or Logout events.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String[]>
        An array of Authentication method Names to register the event for.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Type <String>
        The Type of event to register, either 'Login' or 'Logout'.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EventName <String>
        A unique Name for the event.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        The ScriptBlock to run when the event is triggered.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    4
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ArgumentList <Object[]>
        An optional array of Arguments to pass to the ScriptBlock.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    5
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Register-PodeAuthEvent -Name 'Basic' -Type 'Login' -EventName 'OnLogin' -ScriptBlock {
    
    "User $($TriggeredEvent.User.Name) has logged in" | Out-Default
    }
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Register-PodeEvent
    
SYNOPSIS
    Registers a script to be run when a certain server event occurs within Pode
    
    
SYNTAX
    Register-PodeEvent [-Type] {Start | Starting | Terminate | Restarting | Restart | Browser | Crash | Stop 
    | Running | Suspending | Suspend | Resuming | Resume | Enable | Disable} [-Name] <String> [-ScriptBlock] 
    <ScriptBlock> [[-ArgumentList] <Object[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Registers a script to be run when a certain server event occurs within Pode, such as Start, Terminate, 
    and Restart.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Type
        The Type of event to be registered.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Name <String>
        A unique Name for the registered event.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        A ScriptBlock to invoke when the event is triggered.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ArgumentList <Object[]>
        An array of arguments to supply to the ScriptBlock.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    4
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Register-PodeEvent -Type Start -Name 'Event1' -ScriptBlock { }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Register-PodeMcpToolToGroup
    
SYNOPSIS
    Registers an MCP tool to a group.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Register-PodeMcpToolToGroup [-Name] <String> [-Group] <String> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Registers an MCP tool to a specified MCP tool group.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the MCP tool to register.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Group <String>
        The Name of the MCP tool group to register the tool to.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\># register a specific MCP tool to a specific MCP tool group
    
    Register-PodeMcpToolToGroup -Name 'tool1' -Group 'default'
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Register-PodeSecretVault
    
SYNOPSIS
    Register a Secret Vault.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Register-PodeSecretVault -Name <String> [-VaultParameters <Hashtable>] [-UnlockSecret <String>] 
    [-UnlockSecureSecret <SecureString>] [-UnlockInterval <Int32>] [-NoUnlock] [-CacheTtl <Int32>] 
    [-InitScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>] [-VaultName <String>] -ModuleName <String> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Register-PodeSecretVault -Name <String> [-VaultParameters <Hashtable>] [-UnlockSecret <String>] 
    [-UnlockSecureSecret <SecureString>] [-UnlockInterval <Int32>] [-NoUnlock] [-CacheTtl <Int32>] 
    [-InitScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>] -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock> [-UnlockScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>] 
    [-RemoveScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>] [-SetScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>] [-UnregisterScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Register a Secret Vault, which is defined by either custom logic or using the SecretManagement module.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The unique friendly Name of the Secret Vault within Pode.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -VaultParameters <Hashtable>
        A hashtable of extra parameters that should be supplied to either the SecretManagement module, or 
        custom scriptblocks.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -UnlockSecret <String>
        An optional Secret to be used to unlock the Secret Vault if need.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -UnlockSecureSecret <SecureString>
        An optional Secret, as a SecureString, to be used to unlock the Secret Vault if need.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -UnlockInterval <Int32>
        An optional number of minutes that Pode will periodically check/unlock the Secret Vault. (Default: 0)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -NoUnlock [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the Secret Vault will not be unlocked after registration. To unlock you'll need to call 
        Unlock-PodeSecretVault.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -CacheTtl <Int32>
        An optional number of minutes that Secrets should be cached for. (Default: 0)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -InitScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        An optional scriptblock to run before the Secret Vault is registered, letting you initialise any 
        connection, contexts, etc.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -VaultName <String>
        For SecretManagement module Secret Vaults, you can use thie parameter to specify the actual Vault 
        name, and use the above Name parameter as a more friendly name if required.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ModuleName <String>
        For SecretManagement module Secret Vaults, this is the name/path of the extension module to be used.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        For custom Secret Vaults, this is a scriptblock used to read the Secret from the Vault.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -UnlockScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        For custom Secret Vaults, this is an optional scriptblock used to unlock the Secret Vault.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -RemoveScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        For custom Secret Vaults, this is an optional scriptblock used to remove a Secret from the Vault.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -SetScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        For custom Secret Vaults, this is an optional scriptblock used to create/update a Secret in the Vault.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -UnregisterScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        For custom Secret Vaults, this is an optional scriptblock used unregister the Secret Vault with any 
        custom clean-up logic.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Register-PodeSecretVault -Name 'VaultName' -ModuleName 'Az.KeyVault' -VaultParameters @{ 
    AZKVaultName = $name; SubscriptionId = $subId }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Register-PodeSecretVault -Name 'VaultName' -VaultParameters @{ Address = 'http://127.0.0.1:8200' } 
    -ScriptBlock { ... }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Register-PodeSignalEvent
    
SYNOPSIS
    Registers an event for one or more Signal connections.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Register-PodeSignalEvent [-Name] <String[]> [-Type] {Connect | Disconnect} [-EventName] <String> 
    [-ScriptBlock] <ScriptBlock> [[-ArgumentList] <Object[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Registers an event for one or more Signal connections, allowing custom scriptblocks to be executed when 
    the event is triggered.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String[]>
        The Name of the Signal connection(s) to register the event for.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Type
        The Type of event to register, either Connect or Disconnect.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EventName <String>
        The name of the event being registered.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        The ScriptBlock to execute when the event is triggered.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    4
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ArgumentList <Object[]>
        An optional array of arguments to pass to the ScriptBlock when executed.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    5
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Register-PodeSignalEvent -Name 'Metrics' -Type Connect -EventName 'OnConnect' -ScriptBlock {
    
    "Client connected: $($TriggeredEvent.Connection.ClientId)" | Out-Default
    }
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Register-PodeSseEvent
    
SYNOPSIS
    Register an event for one or more SSE connections.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Register-PodeSseEvent [-Name] <String[]> [-Type] {Connect | Disconnect} [-EventName] <String> 
    [-ScriptBlock] <ScriptBlock> [[-ArgumentList] <Object[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Register an event for one or more SSE connections, allowing custom scriptblocks to be executed when the 
    event is triggered.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String[]>
        The Name(s) of the SSE connection(s) to register the event for.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Type
        The Type of event to register, either Connect or Disconnect.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EventName <String>
        The name of the event to register.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        The ScriptBlock to execute when the event is triggered.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    4
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ArgumentList <Object[]>
        An optional array of Arguments to pass to the ScriptBlock when executed.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    5
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Register-PodeSseEvent -Name 'Actions' -Type Connect -EventName 'OnConnect' -ScriptBlock {
    
    "SSE Connection established: $($TriggeredEvent.Connection.Name) - $($TriggeredEvent.Connection.ClientId)" 
    | Out-Default
    }
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

NAME
    Remove-PodeCookie
    
SYNOPSIS
    Removes a cookie from the Response.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Remove-PodeCookie [-Name] <String> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION

    discard.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The name of the cookie to be removed.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1

    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see

    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Remove-PodeCookie -Name 'Views'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Remove-PodeFavicon
    
SYNOPSIS
    Removes the favicon from one or more Pode endpoints.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Remove-PodeFavicon [[-EndpointName] <String>] [-DefaultEndpoint] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    This function clears the favicon from a specified endpoint, or from all endpoints if no name is provided.
    It safely checks that the endpoint(s) exist and have a favicon assigned before removing. You can also 
    limit
    the removal to endpoints marked as default.
    

PARAMETERS
    -EndpointName <String>
        The name of a specific endpoint to remove the favicon from. If not specified, all endpoints are 
        affected.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DefaultEndpoint [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, only endpoints created with `-Default` will be affected.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    None
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Remove-PodeFavicon
    
    # Removes the favicon from all endpoints.
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Remove-PodeFavicon -EndpointName 'api'
    
    # Removes the favicon from the 'api' endpoint.
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Remove-PodeFavicon -DefaultEndpoint
    
    # Removes favicons only from endpoints marked as default.
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

        Default value

    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,

NAME
    Remove-PodeMcpTool
    
SYNOPSIS
    Removes an MCP tool.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Remove-PodeMcpTool [-Name] <String> [[-Group] <String[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Removes an MCP tool in general, or from the specified group.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the MCP tool to remove.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Group <String[]>
        The Group from which to remove the MCP tool.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\># remove a specific MCP tool from all groups, and the tool itself
    
    Remove-PodeMcpTool -Name 'GreetPerson'
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\># remove a specific MCP tool from a specific group only - but keep the tool itself in case it's in 
    other groups
    
    Remove-PodeMcpTool -Name 'GreetPerson' -Group 'custom'
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\># attempt to remove a non-existent tool, which will do nothing
    
    Remove-PodeMcpTool -Name 'NonExistentTool'
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

NAME
    Remove-PodeOAComponent
    
SYNOPSIS
    Remove an OpenAPI component if exist
    
    
SYNTAX
    Remove-PodeOAComponent [-Field] <String> [-Name] <String> [[-DefinitionTag] <String[]>] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Remove an OpenAPI component if exist
    

PARAMETERS
    -Field <String>
        The component type
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Name <String>
        The component Name
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DefinitionTag <String[]>
        An Array of strings representing the unique tag for the API specification.
        This tag helps in distinguishing between different versions or types of API specifications within the 
        application.
        You can use this tag to reference the specific API documentation, schema, or version that your 
        function interacts with.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Remove-PodeOAComponent -Field 'responses' -Name 'myresponse' -DefinitionTag 'default'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Remove-PodeOAResponse
    
SYNOPSIS
    Remove a response definition from the supplied route.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Remove-PodeOAResponse [-Route] <Hashtable[]> [-StatusCode] <Int32> [-Default] [-PassThru] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Remove a response definition from the supplied route.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Route <Hashtable[]>
        The route to remove the response definition, usually from -PassThru on Add-PodeRoute.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -StatusCode <Int32>
        The HTTP StatusCode for the response to remove.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Default [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the response will be used as a default response - this overrides the StatusCode supplied.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -PassThru [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the route passed in will be returned for further chaining.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Collections.Hashtable[]
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeRoute -PassThru | Remove-PodeOAResponse -StatusCode 200
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Add-PodeRoute -PassThru | Remove-PodeOAResponse -StatusCode 201 -Default
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME

        Accept wildcard characters?  false

        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EndpointName <String>

        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false

INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
