NAME
    Set-PodeSecret
    
SYNOPSIS
    Create/update a Secret in a Secret Vault.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Set-PodeSecret -Key <String> -Vault <String> [-InputObject] <Object> [-Metadata <Hashtable>] 
    [-ArgumentList <Object[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Create/update a Secret in a Secret Vault.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Key <String>
        The Key/Path of the Secret within the Secret Vault.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Vault <String>
        The friendly name of the Secret Vault this Secret should be created in.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -InputObject <Object>
        The value to use when updating the Secret.
        Only the following object types are supported: byte[], string, securestring, pscredential, hashtable.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Metadata <Hashtable>
        An optional Metadata hashtable.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ArgumentList <Object[]>
        An optional array of Arguments to be supplied to a custom Secret Vault's scriptblocks.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Set-PodeSecret -Key 'path/to/secret' -Vault 'VaultName' -InputObject 'value'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Set-PodeSecret -Key 'key_of_secret' -Vault 'VaultName' -InputObject @{ key = value }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Set-PodeSecurity
    
SYNOPSIS
    Sets inbuilt definitions for security headers.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Set-PodeSecurity [-Type] <String> [-UseHsts] [-XssBlock] [-CspReportOnly] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Sets inbuilt definitions for security headers, in either Simple or Strict types.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Type <String>
        The Type of security to use.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -UseHsts [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the Strict-Transport-Security header will be set.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -XssBlock [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the X-XSS-Protection header will be set to blocking mode. (Default: Off)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -CspReportOnly [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the Content-Security-Policy header will be set as the 
        Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Set-PodeSecurity -Type Simple
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Set-PodeSecurity -Type Strict -UseHsts
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Set-PodeSecurityAccessControl
    
SYNOPSIS
    Set definitions for Access-Control headers.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Set-PodeSecurityAccessControl [[-Origin] <String>] [[-Methods] <String[]>] [[-Headers] <String[]>] 
    [[-Duration] <Int32>] [-Credentials] [-WithOptions] [-AuthorizationHeader] [-AutoHeaders] [-AutoMethods] 
    [-CrossDomainXhrRequests] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Removes definitions for the Access-Control headers: Access-Control-Allow-Origin, 
    Access-Control-Allow-Methods, Access-Control-Allow-Headers, Access-Control-Max-Age, 
    Access-Control-Allow-Credentials
    

PARAMETERS
    -Origin <String>
        Specifies a value for Access-Control-Allow-Origin.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Methods <String[]>
        Specifies a value for Access-Control-Allow-Methods.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Headers <String[]>
        Specifies a value for Access-Control-Allow-Headers.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Duration <Int32>
        Specifies a value for Access-Control-Max-Age in seconds. (Default: 7200)
        Use a value of one for debugging any CORS related issues
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    4
        Default value                7200
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Credentials [<SwitchParameter>]
        Specifies a value for Access-Control-Allow-Credentials
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -WithOptions [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, a global Options Route will be created.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -AuthorizationHeader [<SwitchParameter>]
        Add 'Authorization' to the headers list
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -AutoHeaders [<SwitchParameter>]
        Automatically populate the list of allowed Headers based on the OpenApi definition.
        This parameter can works in conjuntion with CrossDomainXhrRequests,AuthorizationHeader and Headers 
        (Headers cannot be '*').
        By default add  'content-type' to the headers
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -AutoMethods [<SwitchParameter>]
        Automatically populate the list of allowed Methods based on the defined Routes.
        This parameter can works in conjuntion with the parameter Methods, if Methods is not including '*'
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -CrossDomainXhrRequests [<SwitchParameter>]
        Add 'x-requested-with' to the list of allowed headers
        More info available here:
        https://fetch.spec.whatwg.org/
        https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/security/cors?view=aspnetcore-7.0#credentials-in-cross-o
        rigin-requests
        https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/CORS
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Set-PodeSecurityAccessControl -Origin '*' -Methods '*' -Headers '*' -Duration 7200
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Set-PodeSecurityContentSecurityPolicy
    
SYNOPSIS
    Set the value to use for the Content-Security-Policy and X-XSS-Protection headers.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Set-PodeSecurityContentSecurityPolicy [[-Default] <String[]>] [[-Child] <String[]>] [[-Connect] 
    <String[]>] [[-Font] <String[]>] [[-Frame] <String[]>] [[-Image] <String[]>] [[-Manifest] <String[]>] 
    [[-Media] <String[]>] [[-Object] <String[]>] [[-Scripts] <String[]>] [[-Style] <String[]>] [[-BaseUri] 
    <String[]>] [[-FormAction] <String[]>] [[-FrameAncestor] <String[]>] [[-FencedFrame] <String[]>] 
    [[-Prefetch] <String[]>] [[-ScriptAttr] <String[]>] [[-ScriptElem] <String[]>] [[-StyleAttr] <String[]>] 
    [[-StyleElem] <String[]>] [[-Worker] <String[]>] [[-Sandbox] <String>] [[-ReportUri] <String>] 
    [-UpgradeInsecureRequests] [-XssBlock] [-ReportOnly] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Set the value to use for the Content-Security-Policy and X-XSS-Protection headers.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Default <String[]>
        The values to use for the Default portion of the header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Child <String[]>
        The values to use for the Child portion of the header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Connect <String[]>
        The values to use for the Connect portion of the header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Font <String[]>
        The values to use for the Font portion of the header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    4
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Frame <String[]>
        The values to use for the Frame portion of the header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    5
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Image <String[]>
        The values to use for the Image portion of the header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    6
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Manifest <String[]>
        The values to use for the Manifest portion of the header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    7
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Media <String[]>
        The values to use for the Media portion of the header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    8
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Object <String[]>
        The values to use for the Object portion of the header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    9
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Scripts <String[]>
        The values to use for the Scripts portion of the header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    10
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Style <String[]>
        The values to use for the Style portion of the header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    11
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -BaseUri <String[]>
        The values to use for the BaseUri portion of the header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    12
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -FormAction <String[]>
        The values to use for the FormAction portion of the header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    13
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -FrameAncestor <String[]>
        The values to use for the FrameAncestor portion of the header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    14
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -FencedFrame <String[]>
        The values to use for the FencedFrame portion of the header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    15
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Prefetch <String[]>
        The values to use for the Prefetch portion of the header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    16
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptAttr <String[]>
        The values to use for the ScriptAttr portion of the header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    17
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptElem <String[]>
        The values to use for the ScriptElem portion of the header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    18
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -StyleAttr <String[]>
        The values to use for the StyleAttr portion of the header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    19
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -StyleElem <String[]>
        The values to use for the StyleElem portion of the header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    20
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Worker <String[]>
        The values to use for the Worker portion of the header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    21
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Sandbox <String>
        The value to use for the Sandbox portion of the header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    22
        Default value                None
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ReportUri <String>
        The value to use for the ReportUri portion of the header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    23
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -UpgradeInsecureRequests [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the header will have the upgrade-insecure-requests value added.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -XssBlock [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the X-XSS-Protection header will be set to blocking mode. (Default: Off)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ReportOnly [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the header will be set as a report-only header.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Set-PodeSecurityContentSecurityPolicy -Default 'self'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Set-PodeSecurityContentTypeOptions
    
SYNOPSIS
    Set a value for the X-Content-Type-Options header.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Set-PodeSecurityContentTypeOptions [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Set a value for the X-Content-Type-Options header to "nosniff".
    

PARAMETERS
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Set-PodeSecurityContentTypeOptions
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Set-PodeSecurityCrossOrigin
    
SYNOPSIS
    Removes definitions for the Cross-Origin headers.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Set-PodeSecurityCrossOrigin [[-Embed] <String>] [[-Open] <String>] [[-Resource] <String>] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Removes definitions for the Cross-Origin headers: Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy, 
    Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy, Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy
    

PARAMETERS
    -Embed <String>
        Specifies a value for Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Open <String>
        Specifies a value for Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Resource <String>
        Specifies a value for Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Set-PodeSecurityCrossOrigin -Embed Require-Corp -Open Same-Origin -Resource Same-Origin

SYNOPSIS

SYNTAX
    Set-PodeSecurityStrictTransportSecurity [[-Duration] <Int32>] [-IncludeSubDomains] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Set a value for the Strict-Transport-Security header.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Duration <Int32>
        The Duration the browser to respect the header in seconds. (Default: 1 year)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                31536000
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -IncludeSubDomains [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the header will have includeSubDomains.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named

        Accept wildcard characters?  false

NAME
    Set-PodeSignalBroadcastLevel
    
SYNOPSIS
    Set an allowed broadcast level for Signal connections.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Set-PodeSignalBroadcastLevel [[-Name] <String>] [[-Type] <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Set an allowed broadcast level for Signal connections, either for all Signal connection names or specific 
    ones.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        An optional Name for an Signal connection (default: *).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                *
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Type <String>
        The broadcast level Type for the Signal connection.
        Name = Allow broadcasting at all levels, including broadcasting to all Groups and/or ClientIds for an 
        Signal connection Name.
        Group = Allow broadcasting to only Groups or specific ClientIds. If neither Groups nor ClientIds are 
        supplied, sending an event will fail.
        ClientId = Allow broadcasting to only ClientIds. If no ClientIds are supplied, sending an event will 
        fail.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Set-PodeSignalBroadcastLevel -Type Name
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Set-PodeSignalBroadcastLevel -Type Group
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Set-PodeSignalBroadcastLevel -Name 'Actions' -Type ClientId
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Set-PodeSignalDefaultScope
    
SYNOPSIS
    Sets the default scope for new Signal connections.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Set-PodeSignalDefaultScope [-Scope] <String> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Sets the default scope for new Signal connections.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Scope <String>
        The default Scope for new Signal connections, either Local or Global.
        - If the Scope is Local, then new Signal connections will only be opened for the duration of the 
        request to a Route that configured it.
        - If the Scope is Global, then new Signal connections will be cached internally so events can be sent 
        to the connection from Tasks, Timers, and other Routes, etc.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Set-PodeSignalDefaultScope -Scope Local
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Set-PodeSignalDefaultScope -Scope Global
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Set-PodeSseBroadcastLevel
    
SYNOPSIS
    Set an allowed broadcast level for SSE connections.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Set-PodeSseBroadcastLevel [[-Name] <String>] [[-Type] <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Set an allowed broadcast level for SSE connections, either for all SSE connection names or specific ones.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        An optional Name for an SSE connection (default: *).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                *
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Type <String>
        The broadcast level Type for the SSE connection.
        Name = Allow broadcasting at all levels, including broadcasting to all Groups and/or ClientIds for an 
        SSE connection Name.
        Group = Allow broadcasting to only Groups or specific ClientIds. If neither Groups nor ClientIds are 
        supplied, sending an event will fail.
        ClientId = Allow broadcasting to only ClientIds. If no ClientIds are supplied, sending an event will 
        fail.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Set-PodeSseBroadcastLevel -Type Name
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Set-PodeSseBroadcastLevel -Type Group
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Set-PodeSseBroadcastLevel -Name 'Actions' -Type ClientId
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Set-PodeSseDefaultScope
    
SYNOPSIS
    Sets the default scope for new SSE connections.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Set-PodeSseDefaultScope [-Scope] <String> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Sets the default scope for new SSE connections.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Scope <String>
        The default Scope for new SSE connections, either Local or Global.
        - If the Scope is Local, then new SSE connections will only be opened for the duration of the request 
        to a Route that configured it.
        - If the Scope is Global, then new SSE connections will be cached internally so events can be sent to 
        the connection from Tasks, Timers, and other Routes, etc.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Set-PodeSseDefaultScope -Scope Local
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Set-PodeSseDefaultScope -Scope Global
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Set-PodeState
    
SYNOPSIS
    Sets an object within the shared state.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Set-PodeState -Name <String> [[-Value] <Object>] [-Scope <String[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Sets an object within the shared state.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The name of the state object.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Value <Object>
        The value to set in the state.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Scope <String[]>
        An optional Scope for the state object, used when saving the state.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Object
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Set-PodeState -Name 'Data' -Value @{ 'Name' = 'Rick Sanchez' }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Set-PodeState -Name 'Users' -Value @('user1', 'user2') -Scope General, Users
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

    Set-PodeTaskConcurrency

        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see

NAME
    Set-PodeViewEngine
    
SYNOPSIS
    Short description
    
    
SYNTAX
    Set-PodeViewEngine [[-Type] <String>] [[-ScriptBlock] <ScriptBlock>] [[-Extension] <String>] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Long description
    

PARAMETERS
    -Type <String>
        The type name of the view engine (inbuilt types are: Pode and HTML).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        A ScriptBlock for specifying custom view engine rendering rules.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Extension <String>
        A custom extension for the engine's files.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Set-PodeViewEngine -Type HTML
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Set-PodeViewEngine -Type Markdown
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Set-PodeViewEngine -Type PSHTML -Extension PS1 -ScriptBlock { param($path, $data) /* logic */ }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

NAME
    Show-PodeGui
    
SYNOPSIS
    Opens a Web Server up as a Desktop Application.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Show-PodeGui [-Title] <String> [[-Icon] <String>] [[-WindowState] <String>] [[-WindowStyle] <String>] 
    [[-ResizeMode] <String>] [[-Height] <Int32>] [[-Width] <Int32>] [[-EndpointName] <String>] 
    [-HideFromTaskbar] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Opens a Web Server up as a Desktop Application.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Title <String>
        The title of the Application's window.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Icon <String>
        A path to an icon image for the Application.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -WindowState <String>
        The state the Application's window starts, such as Minimized.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                Normal
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -WindowStyle <String>
        The border style of the Application's window.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    4
        Default value                SingleBorderWindow
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ResizeMode <String>
        Specifies if the Application's window is resizable.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    5
        Default value                CanResize
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Height <Int32>
        The height of the window.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    6
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Width <Int32>
        The width of the window.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    7
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EndpointName <String>
        The specific endpoint name to use, if you are listening on multiple endpoints.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    8
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -HideFromTaskbar [<SwitchParameter>]
        Stops the Application from appearing on the taskbar.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Show-PodeGui -Title 'MyApplication' -WindowState 'Maximized'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Show-PodeSecurityServer
    
SYNOPSIS
    Show the Server HTTP Header on Responses
    
    
SYNTAX
    Show-PodeSecurityServer [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Show the Server HTTP Header on Responses
    

PARAMETERS
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Show-PodeSecurityServer

NAME
    Start-PodeServer
    
SYNOPSIS
    Starts a Pode server with the supplied script block or file containing the server logic.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Start-PodeServer [-ScriptBlock] <ScriptBlock> [-Interval <Int32>] [-Name <String>] [-Threads <Int32>] 
    [-RootPath <String>] [-Request <Object>] [-ServerlessType <String>] [-StatusPageExceptions <String>] 
    [-EnablePool <String[]>] [-Browse] [-EnableBreakpoints] [-DisableTermination] [-Quiet] 
    [-DisableConsoleInput] [-ClearHost] [-HideOpenAPI] [-HideEndpoints] [-ShowHelp] [-IgnoreServerConfig] 
    [-ConfigFile <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Start-PodeServer -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock> [-Interval <Int32>] [-Name <String>] [-Threads <Int32>] 
    [-RootPath <String>] [-Request <Object>] [-ServerlessType <String>] [-StatusPageExceptions <String>] 
    [-EnablePool <String[]>] [-ClearHost] [-HideOpenAPI] [-HideEndpoints] [-ShowHelp] [-IgnoreServerConfig] 
    [-ConfigFile <String>] -Daemon [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Start-PodeServer -FilePath <String> [-Interval <Int32>] [-Name <String>] [-Threads <Int32>] [-RootPath 
    <String>] [-Request <Object>] [-ServerlessType <String>] [-StatusPageExceptions <String>] [-EnablePool 
    <String[]>] -CurrentPath [-ClearHost] [-HideOpenAPI] [-HideEndpoints] [-ShowHelp] [-IgnoreServerConfig] 
    [-ConfigFile <String>] -Daemon [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Start-PodeServer -FilePath <String> [-Interval <Int32>] [-Name <String>] [-Threads <Int32>] [-RootPath 
    <String>] [-Request <Object>] [-ServerlessType <String>] [-StatusPageExceptions <String>] [-EnablePool 
    <String[]>] [-Browse] [-CurrentPath] [-EnableBreakpoints] [-DisableTermination] [-Quiet] 
    [-DisableConsoleInput] [-ClearHost] [-HideOpenAPI] [-HideEndpoints] [-ShowHelp] [-IgnoreServerConfig] 
    [-ConfigFile <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    This function initializes and starts a Pode server based on the provided configuration.
    It supports both inline script blocks and external files for defining server logic.
    The server's behaviour, console output, and various features can be customized using parameters.
    Additionally, it manages server termination, cancellation, and cleanup processes.
    

PARAMETERS
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        The main logic for the server, provided as a script block.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -FilePath <String>
        A literal or relative path to a file containing the server's logic.
        The directory of this file will be used as the server's root path unless a specific -RootPath is 
        supplied.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Interval <Int32>
        Specifies the interval in seconds for invoking the script block in 'Service' type servers.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Name <String>
        An optional name for the server, useful for identification in logs and future extensions.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Threads <Int32>
        The number of threads to allocate for Web, SMTP, and TCP servers. Defaults to 1.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                1
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -RootPath <String>
        Overrides the server's root path. If not provided, the root path will be derived from the file path 
        or the current working directory.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Request <Object>
        Provides request details for serverless environments that Pode can parse and use.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ServerlessType <String>
        Specifies the serverless type for Pode. Valid values are:
        - AzureFunctions
        - AwsLambda
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                [string]::Empty
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -StatusPageExceptions <String>
        Controls the visibility of stack traces on status pages. Valid values are:
        - Show
        - Hide
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                [string]::Empty
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EnablePool <String[]>
        Configures specific runspace pools (e.g., Timers, Schedules, Tasks, WebSockets, Files) for ad-hoc 
        usage.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Browse [<SwitchParameter>]
        Opens the default web endpoint in the browser upon server start.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -CurrentPath [<SwitchParameter>]
        Sets the server's root path to the current working directory. Only applicable when -FilePath is used.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EnableBreakpoints [<SwitchParameter>]
        Enables breakpoints created using `Wait-PodeDebugger`.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DisableTermination [<SwitchParameter>]
        Prevents termination, suspension, or resumption of the server via console commands.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Quiet [<SwitchParameter>]
        Suppresses all output from the server.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DisableConsoleInput [<SwitchParameter>]
        Disables all console interactions for the server.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ClearHost [<SwitchParameter>]
        Clears the console screen whenever the server state changes (e.g., running → suspend → resume).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -HideOpenAPI [<SwitchParameter>]
        Hides OpenAPI details such as specification and documentation URLs from the console output.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -HideEndpoints [<SwitchParameter>]
        Hides the list of active endpoints from the console output.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ShowHelp [<SwitchParameter>]
        Displays a help menu in the console with available control commands.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -IgnoreServerConfig [<SwitchParameter>]
        Prevents the server from loading settings from the server.psd1 configuration file.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ConfigFile <String>
        Specifies a custom configuration file instead of using the default `server.psd1`.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Daemon [<SwitchParameter>]
        Configures the server to run as a daemon with minimal console interaction and output.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
NOTES
    
    
        This function is part of the Pode framework and is responsible for server initialization, 
        configuration,
        request handling, and cleanup. It supports both standalone and serverless deployments, and provides
        extensive customization options for developers.
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Start-PodeServer { /* server logic */ }
    
    Starts a Pode server using the supplied script block.
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Start-PodeServer -FilePath './server.ps1' -Browse
    
    Starts a Pode server using the logic defined in an external file and opens the default endpoint in the 
    browser.
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Start-PodeServer -ServerlessType AwsLambda -Request $LambdaInput { /* server logic */ }
    
    Starts a Pode server in a serverless environment, using AWS Lambda input.
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Start-PodeServer -HideOpenAPI -ClearHost { /* server logic */ }
    
    Starts a Pode server with console output configured to hide OpenAPI details and clear the console on 
    state changes.
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Start-PodeSleep
    
SYNOPSIS
    A function to pause execution for a specified duration.
    This function should be used in Pode as replacement for Start-Sleep
    
    
SYNTAX
    Start-PodeSleep [[-Seconds] <Int32>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Start-PodeSleep [[-Milliseconds] <Int32>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Start-PodeSleep [[-Duration] <TimeSpan>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    The `Start-PodeSleep` function pauses script execution for a given duration specified in seconds, 
    milliseconds, or a TimeSpan.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Seconds <Int32>
        Specifies the duration to pause execution in seconds. Default is 1 second.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                1
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Milliseconds <Int32>
        Specifies the duration to pause execution in milliseconds.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Duration <TimeSpan>
        Specifies the duration to pause execution using a TimeSpan object.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    None.
    
    
NOTES
    
    
        This function is useful for scenarios where tracking the remaining wait time visually is helpful.
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Start-PodeSleep -Seconds 5
    
    Pauses execution for 5 seconds.
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Start-PodeStaticServer
    
SYNOPSIS
    Helper wrapper function to start a Pode web server for a static website at the current directory.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Start-PodeStaticServer [[-Threads] <Int32>] [[-RootPath] <String>] [[-Address] <String>] [[-Port] 
    <Int32>] [-Https] [[-Certificate] <String>] [[-CertificatePassword] <String>] [[-CertificateKey] 
    <String>] [[-X509Certificate] <X509Certificate>] [[-Path] <String>] [[-Defaults] <String[]>] 
    [-DownloadOnly] [-FileBrowser] [-Browse] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Helper wrapper function to start a Pode web server for a static website at the current directory.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Threads <Int32>
        The numbers of threads to use for requests.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                3
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -RootPath <String>
        An override for the Server's root path.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                $PWD
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Address <String>
        The IP/Hostname of the endpoint.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                localhost
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Port <Int32>
        The Port number of the endpoint.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    4
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Https [<SwitchParameter>]
        Start the server using HTTPS, if no certificate details are supplied a self-signed certificate will 
        be generated.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Certificate <String>
        The path to a certificate that can be use to enable HTTPS.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    5
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -CertificatePassword <String>
        The password for the certificate referenced in CertificateFile.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    6
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -CertificateKey <String>
        A key file to be paired with a PEM certificate referenced in CertificateFile
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    7
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -X509Certificate <X509Certificate>
        The raw X509 certificate that can be use to enable HTTPS.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    8
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Path <String>
        The URI path for the static Route.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    9
        Default value                /
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Defaults <String[]>
        An array of default pages to display, such as 'index.html'.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    10
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DownloadOnly [<SwitchParameter>]
        When supplied, all static content on this Route will be attached as downloads - rather than rendered.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -FileBrowser [<SwitchParameter>]
        When supplied, If the path is a folder, instead of returning 404, will return A browsable content of 
        the directory.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Browse [<SwitchParameter>]
        Open the web server's default endpoint in your default browser.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Start-PodeStaticServer
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Start-PodeStaticServer -Address '127.0.0.3' -Port 8000
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Start-PodeStaticServer -Path '/installers' -DownloadOnly
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Start-PodeStopwatch
    
SYNOPSIS
    Starts a Stopwatch on some ScriptBlock, and outputs the duration at the end.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Start-PodeStopwatch -Name <String> [-ScriptBlock] <ScriptBlock> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Starts a Stopwatch on some ScriptBlock, and outputs the duration at the end.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The name of the Stopwatch.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        The ScriptBlock to time.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Start-PodeStopwatch -Name 'ReadFile' -ScriptBlock { $content = Get-Content './file.txt' }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Suspend-PodeServer
    
SYNOPSIS
    Suspends the Pode server and its runspaces.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Suspend-PodeServer [[-Timeout] <Int32>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    This function suspends the Pode server by pausing all associated runspaces and ensuring they enter a 
    debug state.
    It triggers the 'Suspend' event, updates the server's suspended status, and provides feedback during the 
    suspension process.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Timeout <Int32>
        The maximum time, in seconds, to wait for each runspace to be suspended before timing out. Default is 
        30 seconds.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Suspend-PodeServer
    
    # Suspends the Pode server with a timeout of 60 seconds.
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Test-PodeAccess
    
SYNOPSIS
    Test access values for a Source/Destination against an Access method.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Test-PodeAccess [-Name] <String> [[-Source] <Object[]>] [[-Destination] <Object[]>] [[-ArgumentList] 
    <Object[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Test access values for a Source/Destination against an Access method.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Access method to use to verify the access.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Source <Object[]>
        An array of Source access values to pass to the Access method for verification against the 
        Destination access values. (ie: User)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Destination <Object[]>
        An array of Destination access values to pass to the Access method for verification. (ie: Route)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ArgumentList <Object[]>
        An optional array of arguments to supply to the Access Scheme's ScriptBlock for retrieving access 
        values.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    4
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Boolean
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>if (Test-PodeAccess -Name 'Example' -Source 'Developer' -Destination 'Admin') { }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Test-PodeAccessExists
    
SYNOPSIS
    Test if an Access method exists.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Test-PodeAccessExists [-Name] <String> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Test if an Access method exists.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Access method.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>if (Test-PodeAccessExists -Name 'Example') { }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Test-PodeAccessRoute
    
SYNOPSIS
    Test the currently authenticated User's access against the access values supplied for the current Route.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Test-PodeAccessRoute [-Name] <String> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Test the currently authenticated User's access against the access values supplied for the current Route.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Access method to use to verify the access.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>if (Test-PodeAccessRoute -Name 'Example') { }

NAME
    Test-PodeAccessUser
    
SYNOPSIS
    Test the currently authenticated User's access against the supplied values.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Test-PodeAccessUser [-Name] <String> [-Value] <Object[]> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Test the currently authenticated User's access against the supplied values. This will be the user in a 
    WebEvent object.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Access method to use to verify the access.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Value <Object[]>
        An array of access values to pass to the Access method for verification against the User.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>if (Test-PodeAccessUser -Name 'Example' -Value 'Developer', 'QA') { }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Test-PodeAuth
    
SYNOPSIS
    Test and invoke an Authentication method to verify a user.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Test-PodeAuth [-Name] <String> [-IgnoreSession] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Test and invoke an Authentication method to verify a user. This will verify a user's credentials on the 
    request.
    When testing OAuth2 methods, the first attempt will trigger a redirect to the provider and $false will be 
    returned.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Authentication method.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -IgnoreSession [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, authentication will be re-verified on each call even if a valid session exists on the 
        request.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Boolean
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>if (Test-PodeAuth -Name 'BasicAuth') { ... }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>if (Test-PodeAuth -Name 'FormAuth' -IgnoreSession) { ... }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Test-PodeAuthEvent
    
SYNOPSIS
    Tests whether an Authentication event is registered.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Test-PodeAuthEvent [[-Name] <String[]>] [-Type] <String[]> [[-EventName] <String[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Tests whether an Authentication event is registered.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String[]>
        An optional array of Authentication method Names to check for.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Type <String[]>
        The Type of event to check for, either 'Login' or 'Logout'.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EventName <String[]>
        An optional array of event Names to check for.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Boolean
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>if (Test-PodeAuthEvent -Name 'Basic' -Type 'Login' -EventName 'OnLogin') { ... }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>if (Test-PodeAuthEvent -Type 'Logout') { ... }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS

    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>if (Test-PodeAuthUser) { ... }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

    -Storage <String>

        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,

OUTPUTS

    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Test-PodeCache -Key 'ExampleKey' -Storage 'ExampleStorage'
