NAME
    Test-PodeCookieSigned
    
SYNOPSIS
    Tests if a cookie on the Request is validly signed.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Test-PodeCookieSigned [-Name] <String> [[-Secret] <String>] [-Strict] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Tests if a cookie on the Request is validly signed, by attempting to unsign it using some secret.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The name of the cookie to test.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Secret <String>
        A secret to use for attempting to unsign the cookie's value.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Strict [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the Secret will be extended using the client request's UserAgent and RemoteIPAddress.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Boolean
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Test-PodeCookieSigned -Name 'Views' -Secret 'hunter2'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Test-PodeEvent
    
SYNOPSIS
    Tests if an event type has been registered.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Test-PodeEvent [-Type] {Start | Starting | Terminate | Restarting | Restart | Browser | Crash | Stop | 
    Running | Suspending | Suspend | Resuming | Resume | Enable | Disable} [[-Name] <String[]>] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Tests if an event type has been registered, and optionally with a specified Name.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Type
        One or more event Types to test. If multiple are supplied, will return true if any are found.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Name <String[]>
        An optional list of event Names to test.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Boolean
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Test-PodeEvent -Type Start
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Test-PodeEvent -Type Start -Name 'Event1'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Test-PodeEvent -Type Start, Stop
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Test-PodeEvent -Type Start, Stop -Name 'Event1', 'Event2'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Test-PodeFavicon
    
SYNOPSIS
    Checks whether a favicon is configured for one or more Pode endpoints.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Test-PodeFavicon [[-EndpointName] <String>] [-DefaultEndpoint] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    This function determines if a favicon is configured on a specific HTTP/S endpoint, or across all
    endpoints if no name is supplied. You can also limit the check to only endpoints marked as default.
    It returns $true only if all applicable endpoints have a favicon configured.
    

PARAMETERS
    -EndpointName <String>
        The name of the specific endpoint to check. If not provided, all endpoints are checked.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DefaultEndpoint [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, only endpoints created with `-Default` are checked.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Boolean
    
    
NOTES
    
    
        This is an internal Pode function and is subject to change.
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Test-PodeFavicon
    
    # Returns true if all endpoints have a favicon configured.
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Test-PodeFavicon -EndpointName 'api'
    
    # Returns true if the 'api' endpoint has a favicon configured.
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Test-PodeFavicon -DefaultEndpoint
    
    # Returns true only if all default endpoints have a favicon.
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

NAME
    Test-PodeJwt
    
SYNOPSIS
    Validates JSON Web Tokens (JWT) claims.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Test-PodeJwt [-Payload] <PSObject> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Validates JSON Web Tokens (JWT) claims. Checks time related claims: 'exp' and 'nbf'.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Payload <PSObject>
        Object containing JWT claims. Some of them are:
            - exp (expiration time)
            - nbf (not before)
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Test-PodeJwt @{exp = 2696258821 }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Test-PodeJwt -Payload @{nbf = 1696258821 }

NAME
    Test-PodeMcpGroup
    
SYNOPSIS
    Tests the existence of an MCP tool group.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Test-PodeMcpGroup [[-Name] <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Tests the existence of an MCP tool group.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the MCP tool group to test. If not specified, the function will return true if any groups 
        exist.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Boolean
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\># test if a specific MCP tool group exists
    
    Test-PodeMcpGroup -Name 'default'
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\># test if any MCP tool groups exist
    
    Test-PodeMcpGroup
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Test-PodeMcpTool
    
SYNOPSIS
    Tests the existence of an MCP tool.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Test-PodeMcpTool [[-Name] <String>] [[-Group] <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Tests whether an MCP tool exists, or if it exists in the specified group.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the MCP tool, if not specified the function will check if any tools exist in the 
        specified group.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Group <String>
        The Group in which to check for the MCP tool(s).
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Boolean
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\># test for the existence of a specific MCP tool by name from a default group
    
    Test-PodeMcpTool -Name 'GreetPerson' -Group 'default'
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\># test for the existence of any MCP tools in a specific group
    
    Test-PodeMcpTool -Group 'custom'
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\># test for the existence of a non-existent tool, which will return false
    
    Test-PodeMcpTool -Name 'NonExistentTool'
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Test-PodeMiddleware
    
SYNOPSIS
    Checks if a specific middleware is registered in the Pode server.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Test-PodeMiddleware [-Name] <String> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    This function verifies whether a middleware with the specified name is registered in the Pode server by 
    checking the `PodeContext.Server.Middleware` collection.
    It returns `$true` if the middleware exists, otherwise it returns `$false`.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The name of the middleware to check for.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    [boolean]
        Returns $true if the middleware with the specified name is found, otherwise returns $false.
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Test-PodeMiddleware -Name 'BlockEverything'
    
    This command checks if a middleware named 'BlockEverything' is registered in the Pode server.
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

        function interacts with.

        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------

    Write-PodeHost "The OpenAPI definition is valid"
    }

NAME
    Test-PodeOAJsonSchemaCompliance
    
SYNOPSIS
    Validate a parameter with a provided schema.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Test-PodeOAJsonSchemaCompliance [-Json] <Object> [-SchemaReference] <String> [[-DefinitionTag] <String>] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Validate the parameter of a method against it's own schema
    

PARAMETERS
    -Json <Object>
        The object in Json format to validate
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -SchemaReference <String>
        The schema name to use to validate the property.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DefinitionTag <String>
        A string representing the unique tag for the API specification.
        This tag helps distinguish between different versions or types of API specifications within the 
        application.
        You can use this tag to reference the specific API documentation, schema, or version that your 
        function interacts with.

        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    result: true if the object is validate positively
    message: any validation issue
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$UserInfo = Test-PodeOAJsonSchemaCompliance -Json $UserInfo -SchemaReference 'UserIdSchema'}

NAME
    Test-PodeRequestFile
    
SYNOPSIS
    Test to see if the Request contains the key for any uploaded files.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Test-PodeRequestFile [-Key] <String> [[-FileName] <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Test to see if the Request contains the key for any uploaded files.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Key <String>
        The name of the key within the $WebEvent's Data HashTable that stores the file names.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -FileName <String>
        An optional FileName to test for a specific file within the list of uploaded files.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Boolean
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Test-PodeRequestFile -Key 'avatar'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Test-PodeRequestFile -Key 'avatar' -FileName 'icon.png'

NAME
    Test-PodeRoute
    
SYNOPSIS
    Test if a Route already exists.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Test-PodeRoute [-Method] <String> [-Path] <String> [[-EndpointName] <String>] [-CheckWildcard] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Test if a Route already exists for a given Method and Path.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Method <String>
        The HTTP Method of the Route.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Path <String>
        The URI path of the Route.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EndpointName <String>
        The EndpointName of an Endpoint the Route is bound against.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -CheckWildcard [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, Pode will check for the Route on the Method first, and then check for the Route on the 
        '*' Method.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Test-PodeRoute -Method Post -Path '/example'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Test-PodeRoute -Method Post -Path '/example' -CheckWildcard
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Test-PodeRoute -Method Get -Path '/example/:exampleId' -CheckWildcard
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

NAME
    Test-PodeServerState
    
SYNOPSIS
    Tests whether the Pode server is in a specified state.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Test-PodeServerState [-State] {Terminated | Terminating | Resuming | Suspending | Suspended | Restarting 
    | Starting | Running} [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    The `Test-PodeServerState` function checks the current state of the Pode server
    by calling `Get-PodeServerState` and comparing the result to the specified state.
    The function returns `$true` if the server is in the specified state and `$false` otherwise.
    

PARAMETERS
    -State
        Specifies the server state to test. Allowed values are:
        - `Terminated`: The server is not running, and the context is null.
        - `Terminating`: The server is in the process of shutting down.
        - `Resuming`: The server is resuming from a suspended state.
        - `Suspending`: The server is in the process of entering a suspended state.
        - `Suspended`: The server is fully suspended.
        - `Restarting`: The server is restarting.
        - `Starting`: The server is in the process of starting up.
        - `Running`: The server is actively running.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
NOTES
    
    
        This function is part of Pode's server state management utilities.
        It relies on the `Get-PodeServerState` function to determine the current state.
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Test-PodeServerState -State 'Running'
    
    Returns `$true` if the server is currently running, otherwise `$false`.
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Test-PodeServerState -State 'Suspended'
    
    Returns `$true` if the server is fully suspended, otherwise `$false`.
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

NAME
    Test-PodeSignalBroadcastLevel
    
SYNOPSIS
    Test if an Signal connection can be broadcasted to, given the Name, Group, and ClientIds.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Test-PodeSignalBroadcastLevel [-Name] <String> [[-Group] <String[]>] [[-ClientId] <String[]>] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Test if an Signal connection can be broadcasted to, given the Name, Group, and ClientIds.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Signal connection.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Group <String[]>
        An array of 1 or more Groups.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ClientId <String[]>
        An array of 1 or more ClientIds.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Boolean
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>if (Test-PodeSignalBroadcastLevel -Name 'Actions') { ... }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>if (Test-PodeSignalBroadcastLevel -Name 'Actions' -Group 'admins') { ... }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>if (Test-PodeSignalBroadcastLevel -Name 'Actions' -ClientId 'my-client-id') { ... }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

NAME

SYNTAX
    Test-PodeSignalClientIdSigned [[-ClientId] <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Test if a Signal connection ClientId is validly signed.
    

PARAMETERS
    -ClientId <String>
        An optional Signal connection ClientId, if not supplied it will be retrieved from $WebEvent.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1

        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>

OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>if (Test-PodeSignalClientIdValid) { ... }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>if (Test-PodeSignalClientIdValid -ClientId 
    's:my-already-signed-client-id.uvG49LcojTMuJ0l4yzBzr6jCqEV8gGC/0YgsYU1QEuQ=') { ... }

NAME
    Test-PodeSignalClientIdValid
    
SYNOPSIS
    Test if a Signal connection ClientId is valid.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Test-PodeSignalClientIdValid [[-ClientId] <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Test if a Signal connection ClientId, passed or from $WebEvent, is valid. A ClientId is valid if it's not 
    signed and we're not signing ClientIds,
    or if we are signing ClientIds and the ClientId is validly signed.
    

PARAMETERS
    -ClientId <String>
        An optional Signal connection ClientId, if not supplied it will be retrieved from $WebEvent.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Boolean
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>if (Test-PodeSignalClientIdValid) { ... }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>if (Test-PodeSignalClientIdValid -ClientId 'my-client-id') { ... }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME

        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Type
        The Type of event to test, either Connect or Disconnect.

        Position?                    2
        Default value

        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EventName <String[]>
        An optional name of the event being tested.

        Default value

        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>

INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS

    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------

DESCRIPTION
    Test if a running Task process has completed (including failed).
    

PARAMETERS
    -Process <Hashtable>
        The Task process to be check. The process returned by either Invoke-PodeTask or Get-PodeTaskProcess.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false

INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Boolean
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Invoke-PodeTask -Name 'Example1' | Test-PodeTaskCompleted
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS

    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Test-PodeWebSocket -Name 'Example'

OUTPUTS

NAME
    Update-PodeCookieExpiry
    
SYNOPSIS
    Updates the exipry date of a cookie on the Response.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Update-PodeCookieExpiry -Name <String> [-Duration <Int32>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Update-PodeCookieExpiry -Name <String> [-ExpiryDate <DateTime>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Updates the exipry date of a cookie on the Response. This can either be done by suppling a duration, or 
    and explicit expiry date.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The name of the cookie to extend.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Duration <Int32>
        The duration, in seconds, to extend the cookie's expiry.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                0
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ExpiryDate <DateTime>
        An explicit expiry date for the cookie.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Collections.Hashtable
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Update-PodeCookieExpiry -Name  'Views' -Duration 1800
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Update-PodeCookieExpiry -Name  'Views' -ExpiryDate ([datetime]::UtcNow.AddSeconds(1800))
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Update-PodeLimitAccessRule
    
SYNOPSIS
    Updates an access rule.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Update-PodeLimitAccessRule [-Name] <String> [[-Action] <String>] [[-StatusCode] <Int32>] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Updates an access rule.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The name of the access rule.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Action <String>
        The action to take. Either 'Allow' or 'Deny'. If not supplied, the action will not be updated.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -StatusCode <Int32>
        The status code to return. If not supplied, the status code will not be updated.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                -1
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Update-PodeLimitAccessRule -Name 'rule1' -Action 'Deny'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Update-PodeLimitAccessRule -Name 'rule1' -StatusCode 404
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Update-PodeLimitAccessRule -Name 'rule1' -Action 'Allow' -StatusCode 200
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Update-PodeLimitRateRule
    
SYNOPSIS
    Updates a rate limit rule.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Update-PodeLimitRateRule [-Name] <String> [[-Limit] <Int32>] [[-Duration] <Int32>] [[-StatusCode] 
    <Int32>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Updates a rate limit rule.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The name of the rate limit rule.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Limit <Int32>
        The new limit for the rule. If not supplied, the limit will not be updated.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                -1
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Duration <Int32>
        The new duration for the rule, in milliseconds. If not supplied, the duration will not be updated.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                -1
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -StatusCode <Int32>
        The new status code for the rule. If not supplied, the status code will not be updated.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    4
        Default value                -1
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Update-PodeLimitRateRule -Name 'rule1' -Limit 10
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Update-PodeLimitRateRule -Name 'rule1' -Duration 10000
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Update-PodeLimitRateRule -Name 'rule1' -StatusCode 429
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Update-PodeLimitRateRule -Name 'rule1' -Limit 10 -Duration 10000 -StatusCode 429
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Update-PodeMcpTool
    
SYNOPSIS
    Updates an existing MCP tool.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Update-PodeMcpTool [-Name] <String> [[-Description] <String>] [[-ScriptBlock] <ScriptBlock>] 
    [-ResetProperties] [-PassThru] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Updates the properties of an existing MCP tool, including its description, scriptblock, and/or resetting 
    the properties.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the MCP tool to update.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Description <String>
        An optional new Description for the MCP tool.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        An optional new ScriptBlock for the MCP tool.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ResetProperties [<SwitchParameter>]
        A switch to reset the Properties of the MCP tool, if a new ScriptBlock has changed the parameters 
        which need to be supplied.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -PassThru [<SwitchParameter>]
        A switch parameter to return the updated MCP tool.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\># update the description of an existing MCP tool
    
    Update-PodeMcpTool -Name 'GreetPerson' -Description 'Returns a random greeting to a person, updated 
    description'
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\># update the scriptblock of an existing MCP tool, and reset its properties
    
    Update-PodeMcpTool -Name 'GreetPerson' -ScriptBlock {
        param(
            [string]$Name,
            [string]$Location
        )
        $greetings = @('Hello', 'Hi', 'Hey', 'Greetings', 'Salutations')
        $greeting = Get-Random -InputObject $greetings
        return New-PodeMcpTextContent -Value "$($greeting), $($Name) from $($Location)! via the updated Pode 
    MCP tool!"
    } -ResetProperties
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Update-PodeSecret
    
SYNOPSIS
    Update the value of a mounted Secret.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Update-PodeSecret -Name <String> [-InputObject] <Object> [-Metadata <Hashtable>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Update the value of a mounted Secret in a Secret Vault. You can also use "$secret:<NAME> = $value" syntax 
    in certain places.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The friendly Name of a Secret.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -InputObject <Object>
        The value to use when updating the Secret.
        Only the following object types are supported: byte[], string, securestring, pscredential, hashtable.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Metadata <Hashtable>
        An optional Metadata hashtable.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Update-PodeSecret -Name 'SecretName' -InputObject @{ key = value }
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Update-PodeSecret -Name 'SecretName' -InputObject 'value'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$secret:SecretName = 'value'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Use-PodeAccess
    
SYNOPSIS
    Automatically loads access ps1 files
    
    
SYNTAX
    Use-PodeAccess [[-Path] <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Automatically loads access ps1 files from either an /access folder, or a custom folder. Saves space 
    dot-sourcing them all one-by-one.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Path <String>
        Optional Path to a folder containing ps1 files, can be relative or literal.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Use-PodeAccess
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Use-PodeAccess -Path './my-access'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,

SYNOPSIS
    Automatically loads File Watchers ps1 files
    
    
SYNTAX
    Use-PodeFileWatchers [[-Path] <String>] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Automatically loads File Watchers ps1 files from either a /filewatcher folder, or a custom folder. Saves 
    space dot-sourcing them all one-by-one.

    -Path <String>
        Optional Path to a folder containing ps1 files, can be relative or literal.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------

    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Use-PodeFileWatchers -Path './my-watchers'

NAME
    Use-PodeMutex
    
SYNOPSIS
    Places a temporary hold on a Mutex, invokes a ScriptBlock, then releases the Mutex.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Use-PodeMutex [-Name] <String> [-ScriptBlock] <ScriptBlock> [[-Timeout] <Int32>] [-Return] 
    [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Places a temporary hold on a Mutex, invokes a ScriptBlock, then releases the Mutex.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Name <String>
        The Name of the Mutex.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        The ScriptBlock to invoke.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Timeout <Int32>
        If supplied, a number of milliseconds to timeout after if a hold cannot be acquired on the Mutex. 
        (Default: Infinite)
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    3
        Default value                -1
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Return [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, any values from the ScriptBlock will be returned.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Use-PodeMutex -Name 'SelfMutex' -Timeout 5000 -ScriptBlock {}
    
    
    
    
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$result = Use-PodeMutex -Name 'LocalMutex' -Return -ScriptBlock {}
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS





NAME
    Use-PodePartialView
    
SYNOPSIS
    Includes the contents of a partial View into another dynamic View.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Use-PodePartialView [-Path] <String> [-Data <Object>] [-Folder <String>] [-NoEscape] [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Includes the contents of a partial View into another dynamic View. The partial View can be static or 
    dynamic.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Path <String>
        The path to a partial View, relative to the "/views" directory. (Extension is optional).
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       true (ByValue)
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Data <Object>
        Any dynamic data to supply to a dynamic partial View.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                @{}
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -Folder <String>
        If supplied, a custom views folder will be used.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -NoEscape [<SwitchParameter>]
        If supplied, the path will not be escaped. This is useful for paths that contain expected wildcards, 
        or are already escaped.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    named
        Default value                False
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.String
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>Use-PodePartialView -Path 'shared/footer'
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS

NAME
    Use-PodeStream
    
SYNOPSIS
    Like the "using" keyword in .NET. Allows you to use a Stream and then disposes of it.
    
    
SYNTAX
    Use-PodeStream [-Stream] <IDisposable> [-ScriptBlock] <ScriptBlock> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Like the "using" keyword in .NET. Allows you to use a Stream and then disposes of it.
    

PARAMETERS
    -Stream <IDisposable>
        The Stream to use and then dispose.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    1
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -ScriptBlock <ScriptBlock>
        The ScriptBlock to invoke. It will be supplied the Stream.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    2
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see 
        about_CommonParameters (https:/go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
INPUTS
    
OUTPUTS
    System.Object
    
    
    -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
    
    PS C:\>$content = (Use-PodeStream -Stream $stream -ScriptBlock { return $args[0].ReadToEnd() })
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
RELATED LINKS
